campaignTrail_temp.questions_json = [ { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 717, "fields": { "priority": 1, "description": "It is November 9, 1988. You have been President-elect of the United States for a matter of hours, and everyone and their brother wants your ear. Barricaded in your own living room with Sasso, Estrich, Brountas, and O’Donnell, you have a few decisions to make about the shape of your administration before the wizards emerge from Menlo Park.", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 718, "fields": { "priority": 2, "description": "It’s been less than a week since you put your hand on the Bible, and your first hundred days as President are already in danger of being overshadowed. As Speaker Jim Wright founders under the weight of an ethics investigation over financial misconduct, Democrats are eager to cast him overboard and get moving with your agenda, with the ultimate goal of healthcare reform sometime next year. But that’s still a long way out, and you need to take the little steps first. Fortunately, the Executive Branch is not implicated in this scandal, so you’ve got a bit of leeway here.", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 734, "fields": { "priority": 2, "description": "Over the past decade, defense spending ($303 billion annually as of 1988) has skyrocketed, soaring from about 5 percent of the United States’ total GDP under Carter to nearly 7 percent in some of Reagan’s budgets. From the outset, you’ve vowed to pass defense cuts, both to trim the deficit and fund your own domestic programs. Foley and Mitchell are willing to play ball, but you’re facing headwinds from congressmen wary of losing jobs in their districts. How will you approach this?", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 729, "fields": { "priority": 3, "description": "Last Wednesday, news broke that the real origin behind Senator Joe Biden’s 1987 plagiarism scandal was not the machinations of the Gephardt camp like everyone had assumed, but your own campaign manager and current Chief of Staff, John Sasso. It’s not lost on you that this scandal hits much closer to home than the Wright mess, directly implicating your right-hand man. With your administration’s squeaky-clean image on the line, will you let go of Sasso?", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 732, "fields": { "priority": 3, "description": "Coming out of the Sasso debacle, your administration has decided to change the narrative by moving onto the core problems facing the American economy today. The consistent GDP growth experienced over the past several years has masked considerable economic volatility, with the Dow Jones falling 22% during the Black Monday crash (albeit with a quick recovery) and S&Ls going belly-up left and right as your presidency enters its sixth month. What’s your administration’s plan to tackle this situation?", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 719, "fields": { "priority": 3, "description": "For months now, you’ve been content to keep Gorbachev at arms’ length, micromanaging domestic policy while Mondale runs the State Department with ever-increasing power and autonomy. Among the Secretary’s goals has been to arrange negotiations between you and the Premier over the issue of Nicaragua, where you have established yourself a strong supporter of the Esquipulas Peace Agreement. With elections in Nicaragua scheduled for early next year, what position will you take in these critical talks?", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 756, "fields": { "priority": 3, "description": "During your campaign, a key promise you made was to extend health insurance coverage to the 37 million Americans currently uninsured. The plan you initially proposed was essentially a phased implementation of the model you enacted in Massachusetts, featuring an employer mandate and basic national floor, not too dissimilar from the Kennedy-Waxman proposal. Now that the March 30 deadline you set for your healthcare task force’s report has come and gone, it’s time to pull the trigger on healthcare reform. What’s it going to look like?", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 749, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "If you turn on the television for fifteen minutes these days, you’re bound to find a dystopian film in which Americans toil under Japanese overlords. Indeed, as Japanese investors buy up land on the West Coast and Japanese firms acquire Columbia Pictures and the Rockefeller Center, economic fear and nationalism is the highest it’s been in decades. It is before this backdrop that President Salinas of Mexico has approached you with a request to negotiate a free trade agreement similar to the CUSFTA, spanning all three countries. Is this a project you will pursue in the long-term?", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 728, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q9 Healthcare Plan", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 743, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "As the economy contracts and buckles to close out the decade, Donna Brazile at the OMB has informed you that the deficit is projected to spike past $175 billion in FY91 projections if current spending levels continue. Your defense cuts and efforts to expand the REAP crackdown to a national level can only take you so far, you’re going to have to make some tough choices. Further complicating things is an omnibus reconciliation act going through Congress right now, which would implement PAYGO rules on all congressional spending. What’s your plan?", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 726, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q11 Healthcare Check", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 735, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q12, Midterm Check", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 754, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q13, Iraq Check", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 720, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q14, Congress Check", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 727, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q15, Congress Check", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 745, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q16, Iraq Check", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 723, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "You’re in the office with Lloyd Bentsen watching the Houston Oilers lose to New England. “They always disappoint,” he sighs after a moment, waving his hand. He looks older, more tired. “Anyways, Mike, I wanted to talk to you about the election. I’m getting tired, you know. I haven’t a chance for ‘96. If you… wanted to pick a new running mate, I’d be okay with that, in exchange for Treasury Secretary in your second term.” You nod along.", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 742, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q16, Congress Check", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 724, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "After the coup attempt in August, the Soviet Union entered a terminal stall, shedding nearly a dozen republics as nationalist movements and reformist factions reigned supreme. As the hammer and sickle is lowered over the Kremlin for the last time and replaced by a Russian tricolor, the end of an era is marked, and the United States is now the world’s sole remaining superpower. Paradoxically, U.S. policymakers have worked to prevent this for years, but it does give you an opportunity to flex your foreign policy muscles to the American public.", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 721, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q20, Primary Check", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16487, "fields": { "description": "The Supreme Court has just handed down a landmark decision in the case of Planned Parenthood v. Casey, striking down wide swaths of the 1982 Pennsylvania Abortion Control Act in a major victory for abortion rights. Once more, the band-aid has been ripped off of the hot-button cultural wounds tearing the country apart, and once more, you’re caught in the crossfire. Unless this is exactly where you want to be? Either way, you’re going to have to pick a side." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 722, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "The videotape is nauseating, unlike anything you’ve ever seen. A black man named Rodney King pressed into the pavement, beaten and bloody, barely resisting as a swarm of police officers tase him, kick him, and bash him with batons. Even more shocking is the resulting ruling, three of the four accused officers being acquitted on all counts. Now, racial and ethnic tensions within Los Angeles are boiling over as the situation on the streets spirals out of control, easily overwhelming municipal authority.", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 753, "fields": { "priority": 4, "description": "= Q23, RNC Placeholder", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 731, "fields": { "priority": 5, "description": "= Q24, DNC", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 755, "fields": { "priority": -1, "description": "= Q25, VP", "likelihood": 1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15497, "fields": { "description": "Hurricane Andrew, a Category 5 superstorm, has just made landfall in New Orleans after previously tearing through southern Florida a few days prior. With both regions reeling, will you take time off of campaigning to tour the wreckage and coordinate the relief effort? If you play your cards right, this has the potential to become a real national moment for your leadership to shine." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15546, "fields": { "description": "= Q27, Opponent" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15595, "fields": { "description": "= Q28, Opponent/Debate" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15644, "fields": { "description": "= Q29, Jobs Report" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15693, "fields": { "description": "The days begin to blend together as you travel across the country, the campaign coming nearer and nearer to its close. Is this how things are supposed to end? You’re not sure. Every day Chris and Jack show you a piece of data good for your campaign, only to follow it up with something that seemingly says the opposite. At a certain point, you begin to tune it all out. Where will you make your final stops before the polls open on Judgement Day?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15742, "fields": { "description": "== CYOA ==" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15769, "fields": { "description": "It’s been less than a week since you put your hand on the Bible, and your first hundred days as President are already in danger of being overshadowed. As Speaker Jim Wright founders under the weight of an ethics investigation over financial misconduct, Democrats are eager to cast him overboard and get moving with your agenda. Fortunately, the Executive Branch is not implicated in this scandal, so you’ve got a bit of leeway here." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15803, "fields": { "description": "Over the past decade, defense spending ($303 billion annually as of 1988) has skyrocketed, soaring from about 5 percent of the United States’ total GDP under Carter to nearly 7 percent in some of Reagan’s budgets. From the outset, you’ve vowed to pass defense cuts, both to trim the deficit and fund your own domestic programs. Foley and Mitchell are willing to play ball, but you’re facing headwinds from congressmen wary of losing jobs in their districts. How will you approach this?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16074, "fields": { "description": "= Q9" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15835, "fields": { "description": "As the healthcare fight takes shape, it’s hard not to be disappointed by the ancillary role you’ve played in shaping the debate. Instead of taking your visionary Massachusetts system to the national stage, it’s just become another battle between Democrats and Republicans, the exact type of vicious gridlock you hate. Even so, you’ve expended vast amounts of capital shepherding it through the House over bitter opposition from Republicans and health insurance executives wary of “socialized medicine,” and now the fate of your keystone initiative lies in the hands of the Senate. What’s your plan to seal the deal?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15844, "fields": { "description": "As the battle for healthcare comes to pass, you realize you may have bit off more than you can chew. Your adherence to the Massachusetts plan has invited conservatives and the health insurance industry to put the system itself under a microscope, elevating spiraling healthcare costs in Massachusetts to an issue of national concern. As Dole and even conservative Democrats start to back away over concerns of costs and government overreach, what’s your plan to right the ship and save healthcare reform?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15853, "fields": { "description": "The tsunami triggered by your increasingly bloated, bureaucratic, over-engineered healthcare proposal threatens to consume your entire presidency. The health insurance industry has marshalled the troops in opposition to your agenda, working in tandem with congressional conservatives to stall your agenda on Capitol Hill while funding a nationwide ad campaign to rally the public against healthcare reform. Even Big Pharma is starting to back off, spooked by talk of price controls and regional alliances. As the House vote nears, what’s your plan to salvage this before it’s too late?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16075, "fields": { "description": "= Q11" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15946, "fields": { "description": "The passage of time has yet to dispel the dark cloud that has formed over your presidency since your healthcare reform effort ended in failure. With the midterms looming and no signature reform under your belt, your inner circle has scrambled back to the drawing board in one last attempt to attain a major success before the summer is out. Foley is warning you that this is your last shot before Congress adjourns to defend their seats, so if you want to make your way out of the doldrums before the midterms, this is your last shot to do it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16029, "fields": { "description": "With the midterms looming and your approvals falling, you have elected to head back to Capitol Hill one more time to pad out your résumé before the campaign kicks into gear. Foley is warning you that this is your last shot before Congress adjourns to defend their seats, so you’ve got to make it count." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16076, "fields": { "description": "= Q12" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16077, "fields": { "description": "You knew it was going to be bad, but you didn’t realize it was going to be this bad. Seat after seat is swallowed by the Republican tsunami, Bradley, Harkin, and Kerry all struck down by the THRO revolt. Worst of all, J. Bennett Johnston in Louisiana is toppled by David Duke, to the horror of Democrats and Republicans alike. As Edward King delivers his victory speech in your native Massachusetts, it’s clear that the political winds are changing." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16078, "fields": { "description": "As November arrives, the voters greet it with a resounding rejection of your administration. Of course, Democrats still hold both chambers of Congress, but their margins are significantly reduced, and the House majority is cut to a handful of votes amidst an anti-incumbent revolt from THRO. Nonetheless, it’s clear that the political wins have shifted, and your administration is now on the back foot. What is your reaction to this?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16079, "fields": { "description": "As November arrives, your war room hunkers down, only to be pleasantly surprised by a good year. The THRO revolt falls flat on its face despite your vulnerability, and Senate Democrats are pleasantly surprised to see Rudy Boschwitz of Minnesota unseated by a young professor running an insurgent campaign. Republicans claim a few scattered victories, but there’s no spinning that the conservative wave seems more like a conservative trickle. What’s your response to this turn of events?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16117, "fields": { "description": "= Q13" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16118, "fields": { "description": "Your administration has been blindsided by breaking news coming out of the Persian Gulf, where Iraqi forces under dictator Saddam Hussein have launched a brutal and short conquest of neighboring Kuwait. Democrats on the Hill would prefer that you find a diplomatic solution to the matter, while the nebulous foreign policy establishment seems to think you could benefit from showing some strength here. All eyes are on you, the leader of the free world, to respond." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16127, "fields": { "description": "The dust has yet to settle from that Tuesday night massacre, but you don’t have time to dwell on it. It doesn’t matter if they’re writing your political obituary, you get up and keep going. You don’t know any other way. As the ball drops on New Year’s Eve and 1991 rolls around, you’ve got a State of the Union address to deliver a newly-inaugurated Republican Congress. What will be the topic of your speech?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16136, "fields": { "description": "It’s the Wild West out there. Republicans are mocking your midterm losses, Democrats are mocking them back for failing to retake either chamber of Congress, and you’re just trying to figure out where to go from here. Fortunately, as the New Year’s ball drops and 1991 rolls around, you’ve got a chance to tell the people exactly what you’ve got planned out at this year’s State of the Union address. Now you just have to figure out exactly what that is." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16145, "fields": { "description": "Going into 1991, you have come to preside over two distinct lands. In one of them, you sputter along, barely treading water in the eyes of an increasingly disaffected public. However, recessed within the concrete jungles and marble columns of Washington, there is another country. Here, your sway is considerable and even growing. Now your allies are looking to you to fight for an “assault weapons” ban, introduced into Congress in the wake of the Stockton school massacre. Don’t be fooled by your stature, the mighty NRA is no easy opponent. What’s the move?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16223, "fields": { "description": "= Q14" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16224, "fields": { "description": "Coming out of your State of the Union address, a hapless Bob Michel has been ousted by bomb-thrower Newt Gingrich, the leader of a THRO-emboldened clan of GOP jacobins. After snooping around for any hint of corruption and claiming the scalps of Secretaries Norton and Gray, Gingrich’s cronies on Oversight have zeroed in on their biggest target yet: Walter Mondale. Pointing to your Secretary of State’s alleged campaign finance violations in his race against Gary Hart, the Speaker is raising the specter of impeachment hearings. How do you plan to avert this nationally-televised nightmare for your administration?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16225, "fields": { "description": "As the 102nd Congress is gaveled into its opening session, the Republicans’ failure to take back Congress after two years of President Dukakis has only amplified their fury at their own leaders. This has culminated in the ousting of House Minority Leader Robert Michel, replaced by Newt Gingrich, a partisan bomb-thrower from Georgia. Neutered by Tom Foley for now, Gingrich has promised to rally the troops against you in hopes of flipping Congress and the Presidency in 1992. How will you handle him?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16266, "fields": { "description": "= Q15" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16267, "fields": { "description": "In just a couple of months, Alan Greenspan’s term as Federal Reserve chairman is set to expire, and the economy is still lagging. Your team has largely been operating under the assumption that you won’t reappoint the decidedly anti-growth Greenspan. Bob Reich and Ira Jackson have been very forward in advancing Felix Rohatyn as a better candidate, someone who will give the economy a bump. Will you follow their advice?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16268, "fields": { "description": "As the economy continues to sputter along, you’ve been looking to give it a kick and get it going again. Now, it seems like you have a chance to make something happen. Alan Greenspan’s term as Federal Reserve chairman is set to expire in a couple of months. Greenspan is decidedly anti-growth; you’d love to fire him and replace him with Felix Rohatyn, but you’re under no illusion that Dole would let this fly in the Senate. If you really wanted, you could try to push Rohatyn through, but that requires Republican votes and risks failing. What’s the plan?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16303, "fields": { "description": "= Q16" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16304, "fields": { "description": "Ever since you went to war in the Persian Gulf, you’ve been able to finesse the issue enough to keep it from pulling apart the party, but cracks are beginning to show. Your progressive base thought you were different from the ultra-hawkish Reagan, and on college campuses, they call you a warmonger, plastering images from the Highway of Death on every lamppost, bellowing about your “astroturfed” propaganda campaign to drum up support for military action. How can you keep it together until the election?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16311, "fields": { "description": "Despite the interest you have taken in monitoring the air campaign against the Iraqi occupation over the past several months, it’s unclear how sustainable this is, especially since the Iraqis managed to shoot down an Air Force F-117 last week. The pilot was rescued, but the public has begun to reach the same conclusion long held by General Colin Powell: A bombing campaign alone, however heavy, is insufficient to force a withdrawal and cessation of hostilities. This leaves you a decision to make. With the primary contest looming, a ground war in the Persian Gulf would catastrophically split the party, but letting Saddam off the hook so close to the election wouldn’t exactly look good either." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16318, "fields": { "description": "As the economy continues to shrug off the oil shock created by the Iraqi occupation of Kuwait, a seismic shift has started to form in the GOP ahead of the primary season. Outrage over your weakness abroad has drowned out isolationists like Pat Buchanan, and reports have started to circulate within the Beltway that George Bush has become personally invested in the effort to prevent you from winning a second term. What do you think about this possibility?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16325, "fields": { "description": "A rare split-second of peace in the Middle East over your presidency, however unstable, has opened doors for diplomacy in the region. As the Arab World and Israel turn their eyes to the next millennium and the ensuing diversification of the broader Middle Eastern economy that many speculate will be coming with it, the United States likely stands to gain from a presidential visit. The destination is yours to choose from. Keep in mind that, no matter where you go, the eyes of the media and public perception will follow." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16387, "fields": { "description": "= Q18" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16388, "fields": { "description": "Apparently not content with ripping your cabinet apart on C-SPAN, Gingrich has decided the next lever of power he intends to weaponize is the federal budget process. If you don’t reach an agreement with Congress to fund the federal government by the end of the month, it will enter a shutdown, workers furloughed and services halted. Dole and Gingrich are unblinking, standing by their proposal to shred education and healthcare as the clock ticks down. You have a decision to make." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16397, "fields": { "description": "You can only ignore the issues piling outside your door for so long. Incomes are shrinking, crime and drugs still plague the streets, and trust in government after eight years of your predecessor is still in the gutter. These are the issues that come to mind as you piece together the FY93 budget with Donna Brazile and the OMB. Keep in mind that this is the last budget you will submit to Congress before the election, so people will remember what you prioritize here come campaign season." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16480, "fields": { "description": "= Q20 (Primary)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16481, "fields": { "description": "The Bounty, whose model sits on the Oval Office coffee table, sails once more. You are Captain Bligh, and Jesse Jackson is your Fletcher Christian. Like a phoenix rising from the ashes, he has returned for a third straight bid for the Democratic nomination, presenting himself as a moral, progressive alternative to you. Preaching about the need to restore America’s soul after twelve years of defeats, betrayals, and setbacks, Jackson’s speeches are drawing diverse audiences across the nation. What’s the plan to put down this mutiny?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16482, "fields": { "description": "It’s campaign season again, and with it comes the primary contest. You’ve never had the most captivating personality, and there have long been rumors that a more colorful character would toss their hat into the ring against you. However, after careful behind-the-scenes cajoling and electioneering, you have ensured your uncontested renomination, save for a few scattered rabble-rousers like Lyndon LaRouche and James Traficant. What do you want to do with your time while the Republicans are busy pulling each other apart?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16849, "fields": { "description": "== Q23 (RNC)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16850, "fields": { "description": "Half a league, half a league, half a league onward. Senator Bob Dole takes the stage at the Republican convention in San Diego, flanked by Elizabeth Dole and running mate Carroll Campbell. His speech is flat, dour, and uninspiring, a broadside on the little you have managed to accomplish over the past four years. With everything on the line, how will you hit back?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 16926, "fields": { "description": "You never thought they’d go this far. The extremist, the Holocaust denier, the furthest-right candidate in a clown show primary, is accepting the Republican nomination for President of the United States. Paired with running mate Trent Lott, the two are convinced they can beat you without moderating an inch. You’re determined to prove them wrong." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17014, "fields": { "description": "Taking no chances, the Republican faithful have gathered in San Diego to coronate Jack Kemp, the sunny supply-sider from New York, as their standard-bearer in this election. Combined with the maverick war hero from Arizona, John McCain, the two are touring the country, cracking football jokes and promising the largest tax cut in history. What’s your plan to take them on?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17116, "fields": { "description": "Second time being the charm, Pat Robertson has accepted the Republican nomination in San Diego, hometown boy Pete Wilson nominated as his running mate. Using a Bible as a prop during his acceptance speech, he asks if you’ve forgotten a few key passages. His message is part sermon, part brazen conspiracy theorizing about the “one-world government” you are pushing on America. The whole show is ridiculous and insulting, and you turn off the television halfway through. You’re convinced that this country is losing its mind." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17172, "fields": { "description": "As Phil Gramm accepts the Republican nomination at San Diego, it’s hard not to wonder how a politician even more boring than yourself won the nomination of a major party. He drones on about deficits and interest rates, accusing you of sacrificing long-term economic security for short-term gain. His running mate, a young senator from Oklahoma named Don Nickles, is a little more lively, but still nothing to write home about. How will you approach running against the Gramm/Nickles ticket?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17232, "fields": { "description": "As Bob Dornan accepts the Republican nomination at San Diego, your advisors are ecstatic. He’s a certified lunatic, on the fringe of every issue, repellent to anyone and everyone outside his own base, shouting from the top of his lungs about conspiracy theories. You shoo them away, frustrated and disappointed that your wish for a high-minded civil debate has been denied like this. At least his running mate, Pete Domenici, is a serious thinker. What’s your plan to dispatch these poor souls?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17290, "fields": { "description": "And there he goes. The bomb-thrower from Georgia, the Speaker of the House, the man who has devoted the last two years of his life to making the last two of yours hell, stands behind the rostrum in San Diego, accepting the Republican nomination for President of the United States. Every once in a while, running mate Tommy Thompson waltzes into the spotlight with a smile and a friendly wave, but it never takes long for the Republican solar system to reorient itself around Planet Newt. What’s your plan to consign this man to the dustbin of history?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17365, "fields": { "description": "They are tired of running. Donald Rumsfeld arises from the primaries prepared for the illustrious conquest of America's premier inept coward, you. Accepting the Republican nomination in San Diego, he speaks of a world where demons and bandits roam, free to pillage and kill whatever and however they please. This world, he says, is your world. Joined by Lamar Alexander, they stand as a two-headed beast; one to restore America's status as a military power, the other to restore its heart. How will you open the battle against the technocratic warlord and his pied piper?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17453, "fields": { "description": "= Q24 (DNC)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17501, "fields": { "description": "After some close calls in the early primaries, you’ve consolidated support from terrified Democrats to deny Jackson and his band of mutineers control of the Bounty once more. As you take the stage, the party before you is bitterly divided, disillusioned with your leadership, and doubtful that you can win the November election. What will you say to inspire them to fall in line with you one more time?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17462, "fields": { "description": "After a close call in Iowa, you’ve dispatched Jesse Jackson and his band of mutineers with relative ease and retained the Bounty. Even so, intra-party wounds persist, despite a strong investment overall in the same political project as you. As you take the stage in Baltimore, Maryland to accept the Democratic nomination for the final time, what will you say to the convention hall?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17480, "fields": { "description": "In those days, in those distant days, you promised that the best America was yet to come. Here you stand, four years later, and the best America seems further away than ever. Inside the convention hall, the atmosphere is bleak and dour. Outside the convention hall, protests swamp the muggy streets of Baltimore, shouting slogans about Ruby Ridge and FEMA concentration camps. It’s all so tiring." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17487, "fields": { "description": "Protestors swamp the streets outside the Democratic National Convention in Baltimore, Maryland. They wave signs saying “Say No to the NWO,” “Free Randy Weaver,” and “DUKE: The Butcher of Los Angeles.” Standing at center stage is you, the Commander in Chief, marshaling your party in defense of an increasingly untenable political middle. As you take the stage one last time, what do you have to say for yourself?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17494, "fields": { "description": "Protestors swamp the streets outside the Democratic National Convention in Baltimore, Maryland. They wave signs saying “Say No to the NWO,” “Free Randy Weaver,” and “DUKE: The Butcher of Los Angeles.” Standing at center stage is you, the Commander in Chief, marshaling your party in defense of an increasingly untenable political middle. As you take the stage one last time, what do you have to say for yourself?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17587, "fields": { "description": "= Q25 (VPs)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17632, "fields": { "description": "What to do with Lloyd Bentsen? Although the Democratic bigwigs welcome his return to ensure an open field in ‘96, you’ve got to get over the finish line here first, and the 71-year-old Vice President hardly brings any new bloc of voters into the fold this time." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17649, "fields": { "description": "Al Gore is, for all intents and purposes, a second you. Robotic, intellectual, and deeply technocratic, you’re starting to think that the only difference behind his rallies and yours are the presence of Confederate flags. Ever since the Dukakis/Gore ticket received the party blessing at the DNC, you’ve been itching to slot him into the campaign. But how?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17680, "fields": { "description": "Barbara Mikulski is a good running mate – maybe a little too good. Since announcing her addition to the ticket, she has received an outpouring of support from women, ethnic whites, and labor, and her strong personality threatens to outshine you. With some worrying campaign data about the prospect of a female Vice President, you’ll have to decide how you want to handle her now that the general election is underway." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17699, "fields": { "description": "= Q30 (Visits)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17700, "fields": { "description": "It is Election Day. You’re on campaign in Denver, Colorado, braving through the thin November air in the Mile High City, declaring the death of Colorado’s burgeoning biomedical industry if luddite Pat Robertson is elected President. You stop. There’s a commotion in the crowd. A man pushes through, someone you recognize almost immediately." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17706, "fields": { "description": "You don’t get to have nice things. Fresh off of spreading the asinine rumor that you took medication for depression and anxiety after the Republicans swept Congress, Buchanan has begun to hint at a far more disturbing theory. It’s hard to piece together the whole thing, but it involves a medical decision made by your family on behalf of your comatose brother Stelian after a hit-and-run accident in 1973, a time before euthanasia or “death with dignity.” You don’t even know how anyone got wind of this, let alone Buchanan or the radical right. Election Day is in one week. You have to respond to this." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17719, "fields": { "description": "= Q27 (Rep. #2)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17720, "fields": { "description": "Getting back to the campaign, you and Dole have begun to trade blows daily. Dole is making a point of highlighting the rising crime rates in the wake of the disastrous Los Angeles riots, particularly your emplacement of a moratorium on the federal death penalty. How will your campaign approach the issue of crime?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17738, "fields": { "description": "Let’s get back to the campaign. Recently, Pat Buchanan seems to have found his wedge issue of choice to wield against you: Immigration. “Dukakis spells it out in plain sight,” he shouts on factory floors across the Midwest. “Have you seen the lyrics to his campaign theme song? It says, and I quote, ‘on the boats and on the planes, they’re coming to America.’ And when they get here, they take your jobs and lower your wages.” Your response?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17770, "fields": { "description": "As you and your inner circle meticulously engineer your reelection, John Sasso has been putting his accrued power to good use over the past few months, establishing contact with Republican insiders who brushed elbows with Pat Buchanan during the Reagan administration. Perhaps most damning is an account of Pat Buchanan in 1986, urging President Reagan to visit a Waffen-SS military cemetery in Europe, maintaining that it could not appear that Reagan was “subjected to Jewish pressure.” What will you do with this information?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17782, "fields": { "description": "Getting back to the fall campaign, could there be trouble in paradise for the Kemp/McCain ticket? Last night, the senator from Arizona used what appeared to be a racist remark when talking about his captors in Vietnam, claiming that the term only applied to his captors and that he will hate them for the rest of his life. Kemp has disavowed the comments entirely, and outrage has arisen from the Vietnamese-American community. With internal polling showing that the GOP’s long-running domination of the Asian-American vote in danger of slipping, how will you seize on this?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17801, "fields": { "description": "Let’s get back to the campaign. Convinced that overt religious appeals are the key to victory, Pat Robertson has adopted a fire-and-brimstone cadence, suggesting that you’re not a real Christian since the Bible you swore in on during your inauguration was Orthodox, instead of Catholic or Protestant. He has even claimed that Christians across America have been “tricked” into “falling victim” to your socially liberal agenda. How do you want to handle the religion issue on the campaign?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17821, "fields": { "description": "As you and your inner circle meticulously engineer your reelection, John Sasso has been leveraging his influence to assemble a team tasked with looking into Pat Robertson’s charities. What he has found is nothing short of staggering. Pat Robertson using CBN facilities and mailing lists to promote his campaign. Pat Robertson using his “humanitarian aid flights” to transport diamond mining equipment for his business ventures in Zaire. Pat Robertson using his African timber investments to make deals with warlords. The extent of his corruption is astonishing, so much bigger than you ever thought." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17832, "fields": { "description": "The decidedly boring Phil Gramm has rejuvenated his campaign, traveling through the country like a man on a mission. He seems to have made the federal budget his priority, calling loudly for reducing waste and slashing taxes. His most recent antic is what he calls “the Dickey Flatt test,” asking if any tax increase is worth taking out of the pocket of small business owners like Dickey Flatt, a print shop owner in Mexia, Texas. What do you think of this?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17853, "fields": { "description": "Over the past several months, John Sasso has all but vanished from your inner circle, leading a small task force conducting research into Phil Gramm’s days in business. One evening, he marches back to the Oval Office with a black binder. Inside are records detailing activities from Senator Phil Gramm dating back to the 1970s. Listed in explicit detail are his financial investments in pornographic films, originating with a 1974 film called “Truck Stop Women.” What is your reaction?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17862, "fields": { "description": "Owing to your opponent being Bob Dornan, you’ve adhered so far to a confident campaign schedule, only venturing beyond the Beltway for sporadic visits to the most important of swing states. Meanwhile, Dornan has been barnstorming the Upper South and Appalachia, wielding abortion and “traditional values” as his wedge issues of choice. Your campaign internals tell you the fundamentals are strong, but Chris Edley is starting to worry that, should you not counter more effectively, he might be able to steal some of these votes from right under your nose. Do you believe him?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17916, "fields": { "description": "As you exit a meeting with the Joint Chiefs of Staff, you’re accosted in the hallway by Sasso, who asks for a moment of your time. You take him into an empty conference room, where he produces a manila folder full of documents and tells you a story. In 1978, 46-year-old Senator Pete Domenici entered an extramarital affair with 24-year-old Michelle Laxalt, daughter of his Senate colleague Paul Laxalt, resulting in the birth of a secret child. You feel dirty just knowing about it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17926, "fields": { "description": "You and Gingrich, Gingrich and you, continuing your daily partisan battle from the halls of Congress all the way to the White House, the primary contest but a short intermission. Gingrich has made explicit overtures to the THRO movement, labeling you the “Hypocritical Rascal-in-Chief” and promising to file articles of impeachment the day after the election should you, by some miracle, beat him in the Electoral College. How will you respond?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17942, "fields": { "description": "If there’s one person happy with Newt’s nomination, it’s John Sasso, who has dedicated the past several months to a master file of Gingrich opposition research, compiled since his ascension to the Speakership. Some of it is known information, like his ongoing ethics investigation, other pieces aren’t. His affairs, his financial dealings, his backroom attempts to coup past Speakers, all laid out bare for you to see. As Sasso lays document after document onto the table, you realize you could blow him out of the water if none of it were ever traced to the White House." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17955, "fields": { "description": "It’s a painful comparison you see every night on TV. Rumsfeld is marching from network to network, boardroom to boardroom, and rally to rally, claiming that Neville Chamberlain has been reincarnated. In fact, he won the presidency in 1988. Rummy is running wild with the analogy that you are Chamberlain, who in a naïve and deadly move, surrendered Kuwait to Saddam like he did to the Czechs and the Sudetenland. The comparison is outlandish to some, but frightening to others. How will you respond to the charges of defeatism?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17971, "fields": { "description": "= Q29 (Jobs)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17972, "fields": { "description": "Your campaign has been enlivened by news that this month’s jobs report has easily surpassed expectations, confirmation of your campaign’s narrative of an expanding economy. Unemployment has dropped 0.4 percentage points with a stable labor force participation rate, including a solid rebound in manufacturing jobs and upward revisions of past months. How will your campaign take advantage of this?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17980, "fields": { "description": "Your campaign has been enlivened by news that this month’s jobs report has easily surpassed expectations, confirmation of your campaign’s narrative of an expanding economy. Unemployment has dropped 0.4 percentage points with a stable labor force participation rate, including a solid rebound in manufacturing jobs and upward revisions of past months. How will your campaign take advantage of this?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17990, "fields": { "description": "Your campaign has received disappointing news this morning with the release of an underwhelming September jobs report. Unemployment has decreased by a statistically insignificant one-tenth of a percentage point, and the Dow Jones has sunk 54 points in response to the news. It’s not apocalyptic, but the country just isn’t receiving the economic tailwinds you’d hoped it would. How should your campaign handle this?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 17998, "fields": { "description": "Your campaign has received disappointing news this morning with the release of a dismal September jobs report. Unemployment has remained steady at 6.7 percent and the United States is still hemorrhaging manufacturing jobs, although the labor force is still stable. Nonetheless, the Dow Jones has sunk 78 points in response to the news, and it’s clear the economy isn’t hitting the numbers it needs to recover quickly. How should your campaign handle this?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18015, "fields": { "description": "= Q28 (Debates)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18016, "fields": { "description": "You’re on the debate stage with Pat Buchanan. He’s rattling off statistics about the state of the economy, recounting your legislative failures, and trying to keep the focus of the debate on anything other than the chinks in his own armor. You need to keep on the attack." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18031, "fields": { "description": "You’re backstage before the debate. O’Donnell and Edley are peppering you with bullet points to recite onstage verbatim, Nick Mitropoulous is reminding you about all of Dole’s ticks and habits, and Kitty is trying to push through them all to give you a good luck kiss. You feel strangely detached from the whirlwind around you. How are you going to take him on?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18087, "fields": { "description": "You stop at the podium. You’re restless, tired and nervous from the past month of constant campaigning and debate prep, but on the surface you look calm and ready. Across from you is Kemp, smiling and waving, pointing to someone in the audience. Your expression is dour and serious. You’ve got one shot. How will you take on the congressman from New York?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18176, "fields": { "description": "You’re standing in the elevator, alone with your thoughts as you’re lifted up to the debate floor. It’s nice to have a moment of peace after all the manic prep sessions, surrounded by Chris, Kirk, and Nick, shoving facts and figures down your throat. When the elevator reaches the third floor, what will you say to Pat Robertson?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18237, "fields": { "description": "Phil Gramm’s hand feels weird. That’s all you can think as you walk back to your podium, hoping nobody notices as you wipe your hand on your suit jacket. Truthfully, you’re bored here, but you’ve got to get this out of the way before getting back to the campaign trail. What tone will you take debating Gramm?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18294, "fields": { "description": "You step towards the podium as if you were Plato coming down from the mount, preparing to school a rabble-rouser in the streets of Athens. And perhaps you are, for beside you is Bob Dornan. You flash a weary smile at the crowd, your exhaustion eased by the success you’ve had over the past four years. You turn to the man beside you, staring him down." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18320, "fields": { "description": "You stroll to the podium and shake hands with the swamp creature. A swaggering Gingrich is holding back bile, keeping to his Contract with America, “positive ideas.” You can’t tell if he is certain of his own victory, or just waiting for you to take the first shot. Whatever the case, he’s giving you the initiative. Fire, or let this swamp creature walk all over you one last time." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 18307, "fields": { "description": "The culmination of months of campaigning arrives on this stage, standing across from Don Rumsfeld. The stage lights beam down upon you both like lights from Heaven, signaling an imminent victory or the release of defeat; depending on how you feel after tonight. The questions are generic, but before long the topic turns to foreign policy. The expectation of the question does not numb the sting. This is the turning point of your campaign, for better or for worse. The question is simple: What will you do to continue to project American strength in the world?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 19724, "fields": { "description": "== Duke vs King ==" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 19743, "fields": { "description": "Maybe you’ve gotten a little too good at playing this divided government game. You’ve been one step ahead of Gingrich for over a year at this point, and THRO has lost all confidence in him. Somehow, some way, their quest for an outsider has led them to a new conduit: Ed King. Accepting the nomination at the Republican National Convention in San Diego, the governor’s pitch is simple: “I know how to win, and I know how to beat Michael Dukakis.” As the media eats up the “feud of the century,” how are you going to dispatch this old bastard?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 19734, "fields": { "description": "It’s like you’re suffering from déjà vu. You have no idea how you’re not beating him three-to-one, how it’s not a total blowout, but the man persists, shrugging off his campaign missteps and abysmal record with astonishing ease. The old Baltimore Colts lineman, the genial back-slapping Irishman from Boston, promising to fix this country’s heart, soul, and streets. They’re eating it up." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 19725, "fields": { "description": "You are on the debate stage alongside King, flashing a smile just wide enough to mask your utter disdain for the man before you and his political project. You know for a fact that he feels the same way. How will you handle what will hopefully be the last ninety minutes you ever spend face to face with Ed King?" } } ]; campaignTrail_temp.answers_json = [ { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4411, "fields": { "question": 717, "description": "We’re not making the same mistake as the last Democratic administration. Don’t get me wrong, you’re all staying, but we’re going to be very serious about working with Congress and making sure the national party is in step with our agenda." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4412, "fields": { "question": 717, "description": "Aristotle was the first person to posit that there was a “wisdom of the crowds,” the notion that, should you incorporate enough voices, the mean will be more optimal than any individual response. This will be the guiding decision-making philosophy of my administration." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4413, "fields": { "question": 717, "description": "There are a lot of great minds we’ve surrounded ourselves with over the course of this campaign, both new and old. We have a unique mandate to do something historic, and succeed where other presidents have failed. I expect that you guys will be very helpful in that effort." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4415, "fields": { "question": 718, "description": "I can practically smell the stench of corruption and decay from the Reagan administration. Let’s empower the Massachusetts brain trust to take over and turn this place into an efficient, data-driven organization." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4416, "fields": { "question": 718, "description": "We need to forge a new compact between the American government and people. Let’s travel the country, holding regional development conferences and forging partnerships with local officials. Lloyd can watch things around here while I’m out." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4417, "fields": { "question": 718, "description": "House Democrats want to turn the page? Great. I do too. Let’s get working on a civil rights bill, something that everyone can get behind. It’s awful, how the Supreme Court has been stopping people from suing over employment discrimination." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4418, "fields": { "question": 719, "description": "Gorbachev claims to be about peace, perestroika, glasnost, all that stuff; this is his chance to prove it. This is my final offer to the General Secretary: The Contras are spent. Match us with the Sandinistas, and the election decides the rest." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4419, "fields": { "question": 719, "description": "If we walk into these negotiations and get played, especially this close to the Nicaraguan election, my credibility will suffer for it. All the tanks and guns in the world can’t stop the Sandinistas from losing at the ballot box. Stall the negotiations and let the election go forward." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4420, "fields": { "question": 719, "description": "We’re only hurting ourselves by acting like the Soviets aren’t potential partners in regional development. Peace is not just the absence of war. Let’s press for a summit between myself, Gorbachev, and Latin American leaders to battle disease, unemployment, and illiteracy together." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4421, "fields": { "question": 719, "description": "Mondale’s foreign policy knowledge is second to none, especially insofar as it pertains to the Russians. Delegating this all to him frees up time we should be using to lay the groundwork for our healthcare plan vis-à-vis Congress." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4422, "fields": { "question": 720, "description": "I'm proud to be a member of the American Civil Liberties Union." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4423, "fields": { "question": 720, "description": "I don't share every belief that this group has, but I'm not going to cancel my membership because of some spurious insinuations from the right." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4424, "fields": { "question": 720, "description": "At one time I was a member of the ACLU, but I've been as concerned as any good American by some of the cases that organization has taken on in the past few years." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4425, "fields": { "question": 721, "description": "Null" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4428, "fields": { "question": 722, "description": "Firstly, we need a strong response. But it’d also be a shame if we only defended some of these rich, white areas while the rest of the city is the wild west. We’re going to sweep through with a strong hand and restore order using any force necessary." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4429, "fields": { "question": 722, "description": "We need to establish that anarchy and lawlessness will not be tolerated. I am mobilizing federal troops to contain the violence and assist local authorities while minimizing the risk for excessive confrontation." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4430, "fields": { "question": 722, "description": "If we act rash here and do some heavy-handed response, it’s going to backfire on us. I’m not letting that happen. But I am willing to mobilize troops from the 40th Infantry Division of the National Guard and deploy them to Los Angeles under municipal direction." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4431, "fields": { "question": 723, "description": "That gives me an idea. What about Al Gore? His dad was a friend of yours. He’s got all the best parts of you, known nationally and helpful with the southerners, and he’s a young fellow. Handsome, too, with a good family. And you can have Treasury." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4432, "fields": { "question": 723, "description": "Treasury is yours, don’t even worry about it. And I’ve been thinking about that too. You know what would turn heads? A woman Vice President. Times have changed since ’84; we could really get people talking. What’s the name of that woman from Maryland? The Polish one. Mikulski." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4433, "fields": { "question": 723, "description": "I appreciate the thought. I really do. But I think you do bring a lot to the table. With the, you know, the conservative Democrats, and the South, and Texas. You've done everything I’ve asked of you. I'm not done with you just yet." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4434, "fields": { "question": 724, "description": "It’s paramount that we work to preserve stability in the region. Recognize the Russian Federation and all the republics, work to get them into the United Nations, and give me a personal line to President Yeltsin." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4435, "fields": { "question": 724, "description": "We have a chance to shape the post-Soviet order, the type that only comes up once in a lifetime. Let’s work to expand NATO into Eastern Europe and levy our influence to help them adopt Western-style institutions." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4436, "fields": { "question": 724, "description": "We have a chance to shape the post-Soviet order, the type that only comes up once in a lifetime. Let’s work to expand NATO into Eastern Europe and levy our influence to help them adopt Western-style institutions." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4440, "fields": { "question": 726, "description": "Filler" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4443, "fields": { "question": 727, "description": "We will point out that the Reagan EPA is chiefly at fault for the lack of funds and action in that case, and we'll attack Bush's dishonesty." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4444, "fields": { "question": 727, "description": "I think it would make a good TV ad if we went to Texas and spoke with some of the people who have been put out of work by the oil crash there. Let's ask them how they feel about the Reagan economy." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4445, "fields": { "question": 727, "description": "It's important to remain above the fray and continue with the positive message of our campaign." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4448, "fields": { "question": 728, "description": "Filler" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4450, "fields": { "question": 729, "description": "Don’t forget 1982 that easily. John is one of the best organizational minds I’ve ever met and I’m more than happy to burn some political capital to hang on to him. We cannot do without him." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4451, "fields": { "question": 729, "description": "I’m sorry, John, but you know how the game is played. We can’t send the message that we endorse that kind of behavior in our Administration. You’re a good man, and I deeply appreciate everything you’ve done for us over the last ten years." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4455, "fields": { "question": 731, "description": "Ann Richards gave a great speech during the convention attacking Bush's supposed Texan roots. I think we really have an issue that we can run with here." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4456, "fields": { "question": 731, "description": "I'm skeptical about how someone with Bush's background of privilege could ever relate to middle-class Americans." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4457, "fields": { "question": 731, "description": "I think we would be better served to focus on policy and ideology, than on questions of personality." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4458, "fields": { "question": 732, "description": "There’s no reason not to play to our strengths here. Let’s strengthen the FDIC and FHLBB’s regulatory powers and implement much stricter auditing standards." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4459, "fields": { "question": 732, "description": "Another problem lurking behind all this growth is the deficit. That’s a big one we have to be mindful of. Some of them will fail, that’s inevitable, but we should also do what we can to let these failing S&Ls merge and be acquired privately." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4463, "fields": { "question": 734, "description": "Slash the SDI to ribbons. Scatter it into the wind, get whatever you can from it. Same with the MX missile and the Midget Man. We can get several billion dollars from those programs." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4464, "fields": { "question": 735, "description": "The selection of Dan Quayle as a running mate is deeply concerning, for what it says about Bush himself as much as anything." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4465, "fields": { "question": 735, "description": "We can't focus too much on the second-banana. Voters want to know why I'm better than Bush." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4478, "fields": { "question": 742, "description": "We need to do anything necessary to reduce the staggering rate of murder and violent crime in this country." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4479, "fields": { "question": 742, "description": "I believe, if a state wishes to impose capital punishment, that it should have every right to do so." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4480, "fields": { "question": 742, "description": "I believe that capital punishment is ineffective at reducing crime, and is not applied in an equal manner. I do not support the practice." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4489, "fields": { "question": 745, "description": "I think we can take limited steps to bring this trade back into balance, but in general we should celebrate the expansion of world trade with our allies." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4490, "fields": { "question": 745, "description": "Our first resort should be to negotiate with Japan and take steps to enhance the competitiveness of American industry. But I wouldn't take anything off the table." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4491, "fields": { "question": 745, "description": "We have taken too many steps to expand world trade without adequately protecting American workers from the consequences. I would definitely run a more protectionist Administration." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4501, "fields": { "question": 749, "description": "Right now, the world is solidifying into regional economic blocs. Europe with the EEC, Asia with the Asian Tigers, and so forth. We’re going to make our environmental and labor concerns clear, but a cohesive North American bloc is exactly the kind of thing we need to protect our own influence." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4502, "fields": { "question": 749, "description": "I’m not sure if this is politically feasible right now. To be certain, I’m still highly invested in trade with Mexico, but taking this big a step into free trade and economic globalism right now is probably a no-go. We should demur on this, at least until my second term." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4514, "fields": { "question": 753, "description": "Congrats on making it this far :)" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4515, "fields": { "question": 754, "description": "Unfortunately, the economics of farming cannot support the number of farmers who were borrowing money in the early 1980s." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4516, "fields": { "question": 754, "description": "We need to increase the support for American agriculture by fighting for exports in our trade deals -- with the Soviet Union, Mexico, and elsewhere." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4517, "fields": { "question": 754, "description": "We could have stabilized this situation much earlier with aggressive intervention from the federal government. This is the type of thing I would have done if I were President." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4518, "fields": { "question": 755, "description": "The Midwest. Especially Illinois, Michigan, and Ohio." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4519, "fields": { "question": 755, "description": "Texas and its neighboring states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4520, "fields": { "question": 755, "description": "California." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4521, "fields": { "question": 755, "description": "New York, New Jersey, and Pennsylvania." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4523, "fields": { "question": 756, "description": "If I said it was similar to the Kennedy-Waxman bill, that’s because it is. Let’s use that as a crutch and drop some of the ideas we had about leveraging state administration to implement coverage nationally." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4524, "fields": { "question": 756, "description": "We’ve got to keep it simple. The Massachusetts plan is so great because it plays into the strengths of our existing systems—private insurance and state-by-state implementation—instead of trying to undo and replace them all. Take that, and scale it up." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 4525, "fields": { "question": 756, "description": "Cracks are already starting to show up in Massachusetts. Let’s hand these blueprints over to the brain trust and see if they can make something that really works on a national scale. Something that won’t spook big business. Managed competition." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15547, "fields": { "question": 15546, "description": "The Midwest. Especially Illinois, Michigan, and Ohio." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15564, "fields": { "question": 15546, "description": "Texas and its neighboring states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15577, "fields": { "question": 15546, "description": "California." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15584, "fields": { "question": 15546, "description": "New York, New Jersey, and Pennsylvania." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15596, "fields": { "question": 15595, "description": "The Midwest. Especially Illinois, Michigan, and Ohio." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15613, "fields": { "question": 15595, "description": "Texas and its neighboring states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15626, "fields": { "question": 15595, "description": "California." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15633, "fields": { "question": 15595, "description": "New York, New Jersey, and Pennsylvania." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15645, "fields": { "question": 15644, "description": "The Midwest. Especially Illinois, Michigan, and Ohio." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15662, "fields": { "question": 15644, "description": "Texas and its neighboring states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15675, "fields": { "question": 15644, "description": "California." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15682, "fields": { "question": 15644, "description": "New York, New Jersey, and Pennsylvania." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15746, "fields": { "question": 718, "description": "Everyone knows I’m not exactly a foreign policy heavyweight, that’s why we have Mondale running State and Albright in New York. But it would probably be good to travel out to some of our NATO allies and let them know we’ve still got their back." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15750, "fields": { "question": 734, "description": "Just lower the topline defense appropriation. Let the Pentagon figure out what to cut and what to keep. See if we can scrounge together four billion dollars." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15752, "fields": { "question": 734, "description": "I think we can get away with quite a bit here. We’ve got a good mandate, and we’ve already cultivated a good relationship with Congress. I think we can lower the topline defense appropriation to the tune of about seven billion dollars." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15754, "fields": { "question": 734, "description": "Let’s push for another round of BRAC closures. Pair them with personnel reductions, so that we can shrink active duty troop numbers. That seems like the most up-front way to go about doing this, and the savings will only increase in the long-term." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15758, "fields": { "question": 732, "description": "This is a good opportunity to push for structural economic reform. We can temporarily federally guarantee some of these, tie bank recapitalization to community investment, and include regional development incentives for areas being hit by the deindustrialization collapse." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15761, "fields": { "question": 732, "description": "I’ve heard a few interesting ideas about this from Ira Jackson and Felix Rohatyn. Let’s create a federally backed, technology-driven monitoring system to track bank solvency in real time; let’s also introduce risk-weighted deposit insurance premiums to disincentivize recklessness." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15770, "fields": { "question": 15769, "description": "I can practically smell the stench of corruption and decay from the Reagan administration. Let’s empower the Massachusetts brain trust to take over and turn this place into an efficient, data-driven organization." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15774, "fields": { "question": 15769, "description": "We need to forge a new compact between the American government and people. Let’s travel the country, holding regional development conferences and forging partnerships with local officials. Lloyd can watch things around here while I’m out." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15778, "fields": { "question": 15769, "description": "House Democrats want to turn the page? Great. I do too. Let’s get working on a civil rights bill, something that everyone can get behind. It’s awful, how the Supreme Court has been stopping people from suing over employment discrimination." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15781, "fields": { "question": 15769, "description": "Everyone knows I’m not exactly a foreign policy heavyweight, that’s why we have Mondale running State and Albright in New York. But it would probably be good to travel out to some of our NATO allies and let them know we’ve still got their back." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15804, "fields": { "question": 15803, "description": "Slash the SDI to ribbons. Scatter it into the wind, get whatever you can from it. Same with the MX missile and the Midget Man. We can get several billion dollars from those programs." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15816, "fields": { "question": 15803, "description": "Just lower the topline defense appropriation. Let the Pentagon figure out what to cut and what to keep. See if we can scrounge together four billion dollars." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15820, "fields": { "question": 15803, "description": "I think we can get away with quite a bit here. We’ve got a good mandate, and we’ve already cultivated a good relationship with Congress. I think we can lower the topline defense appropriation to the tune of about seven billion dollars." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15824, "fields": { "question": 15803, "description": "Let’s push for another round of BRAC closures. Pair them with personnel reductions, so that we can shrink active duty troop numbers. That seems like the most up-front way to go about doing this, and the savings will only increase in the long-term." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15836, "fields": { "question": 15835, "description": "I’ll be the first to admit that Capitol Hill isn’t my domain. Mitchell, Cranston, and the like are more in their element there. The real power of the presidency, in this respect, is the bully pulpit. Let’s cross the country delivering public speeches to advocate for healthcare reform." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15838, "fields": { "question": 15835, "description": "The real power center in the Democratic caucus isn’t Mitchell, it’s Byrd. His knowledge of Senate inside baseball is second to none, and he’s got credibility with a lot of those boll weevil swing votes that I don’t. Let’s invite him over to the Oval and see if we can find common ground." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15840, "fields": { "question": 15835, "description": "It’s about compromise, isn’t it? All one hundred senators sincerely want what’s best for this country, they just disagree on how to go about doing it. Let’s reach out to some moderate Republicans and conservative Democrats in good faith and see what they have to say." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15842, "fields": { "question": 15835, "description": "This thing has basically no chance of clearing the filibuster if it just becomes another left versus right battle. We’d probably have a better chance of achieving substantive reform if we abandoned the bill as-is and pursued the most important provisions individually. " } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15845, "fields": { "question": 15844, "description": "When I was governor, there was no problem. It was only after I assumed the presidency that my successor scuttled the state budget for political gain. We’ve learned a lot from our experience working on this in Boston, and we’re confident we can make this work on the national stage." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15847, "fields": { "question": 15844, "description": "Forget Dole, we don‘t need him. I never expected many Republicans to cross over for us, it’s the boll weevils who are giving us grief, people like Boren and Shelby, who we really need to put the pressure on. Make ‘em sweat." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15849, "fields": { "question": 15844, "description": "I don’t see any reason why we can’t try to meet somewhere in the middle. We have a lot of more ambitious proposals in this plan, things like the employer mandate, that we can use as bargaining chips to get the rest of the thing through." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15851, "fields": { "question": 15844, "description": "What we’re facing is an optics problem. Every individual provision in this bill has a +20 approval rating, but as soon as you slap my name on it, it’s ten points underwater. Why don’t we play it smartly? Abandon the bill itself, but enact the key points on their own. The effect is the same. " } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15854, "fields": { "question": 15853, "description": "We can’t compete with these Harry and Louise ads running wall-to-wall. We’ve got to play their game, win over hearts and minds. These schmucks on Capitol Hill will only vote for this thing if they think they’ll lose their seats if they don’t." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15856, "fields": { "question": 15853, "description": "Clearing the filibuster is the only way we’re going to get what we want out of this thing. I don’t care what you do or how long it takes, as long as this thing gets back to my desk with sixty votes in the Senate. Find two Republicans willing to break ranks, it can’t be too difficult." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15858, "fields": { "question": 15853, "description": "Let’s not complicate this too much. We can push it through in the budget bill using reconciliation. It’ll expose our plan to more negotiations and the ability to be watered down, but we can’t let perfect be the enemy of good enough." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15860, "fields": { "question": 15853, "description": "Table it. This thing is toxic, and if we tie our fortunes to it, it’ll drag us down with it. Take from it all the most passable ideas, things like catastrophic healthcare with prescription drug benefits, and put them through on their own." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15876, "fields": { "question": 15853, "description": "Clearing the filibuster is the only way we’re going to get what we want out of this thing. I don’t care what you do or how long it takes, as long as this thing gets back to my desk with sixty votes in the Senate. Find two Republicans willing to break ranks, it can’t be too difficult." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15891, "fields": { "question": 15844, "description": "Forget Dole, we don‘t need him. I never expected many Republicans to cross over for us, it’s the boll weevils who are giving us grief, people like Boren and Shelby, who we really need to put the pressure on. Make ‘em sweat." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15895, "fields": { "question": 15844, "description": "I don’t see any reason why we can’t try to meet somewhere in the middle. We have a lot of more ambitious proposals in this plan, things like the employer mandate, that we can use as bargaining chips to get the rest of the thing through." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15898, "fields": { "question": 15835, "description": "The real power center in the Democratic caucus isn’t Mitchell, it’s Byrd. His knowledge of Senate inside baseball is second to none, and he’s got credibility with a lot of those boll weevil swing votes that I don’t. Let’s invite him over to the Oval and see if we can find common ground." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15901, "fields": { "question": 15835, "description": "It’s about compromise, isn’t it? All one hundred senators sincerely want what’s best for this country, they just disagree on how to go about doing it. Let’s reach out to some moderate Republicans and conservative Democrats in good faith and see what they have to say." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 15922, "fields": { "question": 743, "description": "PAYGO is exactly the type of thing we need to get our fiscal house in order.
