He’s not invincible. Remember the rampant fraud and bribery in Ed King’s Revenue Department? Remember how the ringleader, Stan Barczak, had King bumper stickers in his trunk? How deep does this really go?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19737, "fields": { "question": 19734, "description": "He’s just old. In both ideas and age, but don’t bring up the latter. He’s almost seventy. The Baltimore Colts don’t even exist anymore. Let’s campaign as much as possible and dare him to keep up with the Marathon Man." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19739, "fields": { "question": 19734, "description": "This whole thing he does, where he presents himself as this everyday guy, it’s total nonsense. Remember all those expenses when he was governor? Thousands and thousands of taxpayer dollars on lavish lobster salads, restaurant meals for his entire staff. If you want to balance the budget, you’ve got to be frugal." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19741, "fields": { "question": 19734, "description": "What would someone with the temperament of Ed King do as President? He’s an authoritarian, a cigar-chomping kleptocrat. He uses an escort of jackbooted state troopers as governor. He tried to silence his critics with lawsuits. That’s not leadership. It’s intimidation." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19744, "fields": { "question": 19743, "description": "He was one of the worst governors in Massachusetts history. His Secretary of Transportation is behind bars for corruption. He was so awful that I had to come back and kick him out of office. It’d be a crying shame if this man became President." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19746, "fields": { "question": 19743, "description": "They’re obsessed with me. All they care about is me. They don’t care about helping the middle class, or creating jobs, or protecting global security. They’re trying to make this about Massachusetts ten years ago when it’s really about America ten years from now. I’m focused on the future." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19748, "fields": { "question": 19743, "description": "Geez. I mean, this guy, they called him Reagan’s favorite Democrat, when he was one, for good reason. He’s really a right-wing radical. And his running mate, John Ashcroft? He’s a Federalist Society nutcase. He’d throw women in jail for seeking an abortion." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19750, "fields": { "question": 19743, "description": "The choice here is easy because you can observe how we treated identical executive offices. I transformed the state, balancing the budget without raising taxes and introducing a visionary welfare-to-work program. All he did was slash programs and line the pockets of his buddies." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19859, "fields": { "question": 16482, "description": "Okay, look, don’t get too cheeky. Everything’s going to be business as usual at the White House. Seeing me as America’s president, getting things done and taking care of the business of government, is worth far more than campaign ads or whistle-stop tours." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19862, "fields": { "question": 16225, "description": "Here’s the thing with Newt. He really gives us the best of both worlds. He’s loony, a living example of right-wing radicalism, but he’s also harmless to our agenda, at least as long as Foley runs the place. Let’s really go at it with him and draw him into a fight, so that people know who we’re up against." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19865, "fields": { "question": 16225, "description": "Try and play them against each other. These “old guard” types in the Senate, Dole and Simpson, they hate the dickens out of Newt. We could pry them apart on some of these critical issues, maybe the moderate ones could help us break the filibuster." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19870, "fields": { "question": 15497, "description": "Vice President Bentsen has valuable local connections in the South, particularly Louisiana, and could really help get relief to the areas. He’s going to manage the relief effort while I campaign in key swing states nationally." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19879, "fields": { "question": 16926, "description": "Geez, have you seen the ideas he’s proposing? They’re more than authoritarian, they’re totalitarian. He’d be a dictator. Compare him to Stalin or Mussolini. Hell, compare him to Big Brother, from Orwell’s book. Just scare the crap out of voters." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer", "pk": 19933, "fields": { "question": 16029, "description": "I’ve seen a few of the studies published by Bob Reich about how much we could save in overhead costs if we established a body to coordinate infrastructure across the federal departments and with the states. Let’s sign an EO establishing the National Infrastructure Council." } } ]; campaignTrail_temp.states_json = [ { "model": "campaign_trail.state", "pk": 456, "fields": { "name": "Alabama", "abbr": "AL", "Washington", "abbr": "WA", "electoral_votes": 11, "popular_votes": 2287565, "poll_closing_time": 300, "winner_take_all_flg": 1, "election": 9 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.state", "pk": 503, }, { "model": "campaign_trail.state", "pk": 505, "fields": { "name": "Wisconsin", "abbr": "WI", "electoral_votes": 11, "popular_votes": 2531114, "poll_closing_time": 180, "winner_take_all_flg": 1, "election": 9 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.state", "pk": 506, "fields": { "name": "Wyoming", "abbr": "WY", "electoral_votes": 3, "popular_votes": 199884, "poll_closing_time": 180, "winner_take_all_flg": 1, "election": 9 } } ]; campaignTrail_temp.issues_json = [ { "model": "campaign_trail.issue", "pk": 57, "fields": { "name": "Economy", "description": "", "stance_1": "Blade Runner", "stance_desc_1": "These backwater rurals may look nothing like the corporate dystopia in Blade Runner, but with the policies of unchecked, hyper-individualist crony capitalism they promote, the end result won’t be all that different.", "stance_2": "Trickle-Down", "stance_desc_2": "Just turn back the clock to the Reagan years. More tax cuts, less regulation, and less government spending. The wealth will trickle down, I’m sure of it. Eventually.", "stance_3": "Workfare", "stance_desc_3": "Every night, these guys pray before bed that you will enact the same work-based welfare reform nationally as you did in Massachusetts. Otherwise, they’re moderate unifiers.", "stance_4": "Indecisive", "stance_desc_4": " A growing number of Americans are just indifferent to the nitty gritty of economic policy. They don’t care what you do as long as the line goes up and groceries are cheaper.", "stance_5": "Deficit Hawks", "stance_desc_5": "For many years, fiscal responsibility was a calling card for economic conservatives. However, as the national debt grew threefold under your predecessor, austerity became a leading issue for mainstream Democrats.", "stance_6": "Pro-Union", "stance_desc_6": "A significant part of the country feels left out by the Reagan-era boom, no matter how personally popular the Gipper remains. There are still wide swaths of the northeast and industrial midwest where labor unions are as American as apple pie.", "stance_7": "Welfare State", "stance_desc_7": "The few doctrinaire welfarists left after the onslaught of the 70s and 80s are concentrated in the highly educated, progressive cities and universities spanning the north and west.", "election": 15 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.issue", "pk": 58, "fields": { "name": "Crime", "description": "", "stance_1": "Broken Windows", "stance_desc_1": "“Please, God, let us send the army into American cities, nothing else matters. I’ll do anything, please. I just want to see skulls cracked with batons and billy clubs one more time before I die.”", "stance_2": "Broken Windows", "stance_desc_2": "“Please, God, let us send the army into American cities, nothing else matters. I’ll do anything, please. I just want to see skulls cracked with batons and billy clubs one more time before I die.”", "stance_3": "Law and Order", "stance_desc_3": "As crime rates have risen in recent years, these kinds of voters have come to form a majority of the country. Typically concentrated in the suburbs, they’ve long since fled the city to their sanctuaries of racial homogeneity.", "stance_4": "Mixed", "stance_desc_4": "As with any issue, the fence-sitters remain a valuable group of voters in the crime debate. Alienated by the right’s hardcore rhetoric on the issue as well as the left’s softness, they don’t care what you do to curb the crime, as long as it works.", "stance_5": "It's The Guns", "stance_desc_5": "Like all Americans, these kinds of people want to prevent crime, but in addition to tougher policies, they support progressive initiatives to keep assault weapons off the streets and out of the hands of would-be criminals. They are also the sworn enemy of the NRA.", "stance_6": "Social Problem", "stance_desc_6": "Made uncomfortable by the violent, often dehumanizing rhetoric of the tough-on-crime crowd, these voters adamantly claim that crime is not a result of innate qualities, but because of material conditions, and that the easiest way to reduce crime is to stop it before it happens.", "stance_7": "Social Problem", "stance_desc_7": "Made uncomfortable by the violent, often dehumanizing rhetoric of the tough-on-crime crowd, these voters adamantly claim that crime is not a result of innate qualities, but because of material conditions, and that the easiest way to reduce crime is to stop it before it happens.", "election": 15 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.issue", "pk": 59, "fields": { "name": "Culture", "description": "", "stance_1": "Deliverance", "stance_desc_1": "Welcome to the world of rural isolation, where meth labs outnumber post offices. Hillbilly country largely leans conservative, but Democrats have enjoyed a near-monopoly on the Appalachian region since you were a child. Could the winds change?", "stance_2": "Family Values", "stance_desc_2": "Megachurches, culture wars, and a strong belief that America’s decline can be reversed through prayer and discipline. Mobilized to the right by Falwell and the Moral Majority starting in 1980.", "stance_3": "American Heartland", "stance_desc_3": "Devastated by the farm crisis, you made historic gains across the American heartland in ‘88. Polite, hardworking, and traditional, these poor souls are only remembered once every four years.", "stance_4": "Blue-Collar", "stance_desc_4": "Once the cornerstone of the Democratic coalition, the rise of the New Left and, later, the Reagan Revolution led many of these voters (particularly white ethnics) to defect to the Republicans.", "stance_5": "Sprawling Suburbia", "stance_desc_5": "The Republican grip on suburbia is slowly but surely starting to erode. Endless cul-de-sacs, minivans, and chain restaurants stretch out to the horizon, their residents comfortably numb and deeply skeptical of anything “weird.”", "stance_6": "Cosmopolitan Yuppies", "stance_desc_6": "Living in condos and chasing careers, these young, educated professionals believe the future is bright, and more importantly, theirs. Won over by the pro-market optimism of Ronald Reagan, they could defect to you en masse if the GOP leans hard into populism and culture wars.", "stance_7": "Coastal Elites", "stance_desc_7": "The world of finance, media, and politics, where glass towers rise over tent cities. Everyone is either running the country, or thinks they will be in ten years’ time.", "election": 15 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.issue", "pk": 60, "fields": { "name": "Disposition", "description": "", "stance_1": "Populist ", "stance_desc_1": "Decades of abandonment and disfavorance have left these regions reeling, betrayed by the American dream they so thoroughly believed in. Some of them flipped to you in ‘88, but should a demagogue or populist rise up against you, his magnetism in these areas would be undeniable.", "stance_2": "Populist ", "stance_desc_2": "Decades of abandonment and disfavorance have left these regions reeling, betrayed by the American dream they so thoroughly believed in. Some of them flipped to you in ‘88, but should a demagogue or populist rise up against you, his magnetism in these areas would be undeniable.", "stance_3": "Lean Populist ", "stance_desc_3": "In many regions, the people haven’t been feeling the boom promised to them over the past decade. As their communities hemorrhage jobs and their farms go bust, they want someone who will speak for them and change the way things are going.", "stance_4": "Ambivalent", "stance_desc_4": "Many people fall somewhere in the middle of the “establishment-populist” debate, seeing through demagogic bloviating without being particularly inspired by the flat, unemotional way of politics preached by the Democratic Leadership Coalition.", "stance_5": "Lean Technocrat", "stance_desc_5": "As technology advances and the Democrats pivot shamelessly towards the center, many disaffected, affluent Reagan voters have been won over by the “competence” camp in recent years, especially since your rise to power.", "stance_6": "Technocrat", "stance_desc_6": "These voters have long rejected radical upheaval as the answer to society’s ailments. They instead embrace data-driven, moderate solutions, trusting competent experts to manage the country’s problems.", "stance_7": "Technocrat", "stance_desc_7": "These voters have long rejected radical upheaval as the answer to society’s ailments. They instead embrace data-driven, moderate solutions, trusting competent experts to manage the country’s problems.", "election": 15 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.issue", "pk": 61, "fields": { "name": "Ethnic Vote", "description": "", "stance_1": "White Catholic", "stance_desc_1": "The most prominent ethnic vote here is that of white Catholics. Hailing from countries like Ireland, Italy, and Poland, these voters can often be won over with a mix of traditional values, anti-crime messaging, and pro-worker policies.", "stance_2": "Hispanic/Latino", "stance_desc_2": "A handful of states in the southwest have significant Latino minorities, many of whom are of Mexican descent. These voters dislike nativism and respond to practical, compassionate social policies, especially labor rights.", "stance_3": "Inuit", "stance_desc_3": "Up in the last frontier, the Inuit people make up a good fifth of the state’s voting population. Although Alaska is probably a longshot this time, their turnout could still be juiced by strong stances on healthcare and the environment.", "stance_4": "White Protestant", "stance_desc_4": "Including the WASP old stock as well as more recent German and Scandinavian arrivals, this diffuse demographic often lacks the cohesiveness that makes the ethnic vote so critical. Even so, there are through-lines like stability and moderate politics.", "stance_5": "Pluralistic", "stance_desc_5": "These diverse states have so many competing ethnic blocs — Catholic, Jewish, Latino, African-American, and Asian-American — that they afford you several different lanes to victory via ethnic politics. Coalition management is key.", "stance_6": "African-American", "stance_desc_6": "African-Americans feel vaguely positive towards you, but there was little enthusiasm for you among their communities in ‘88. If you make bold commitments to economic reform that address the problems they face, they’d be sure to return the favor next time.", "stance_7": "???", "stance_desc_7": "Joseph Smith was called a prophet, dum dum dum dum dum!", "election": 15 } } ]; campaignTrail_temp.state_issue_score_json = [ { "model": "campaign_trail.state_issue_score", "pk": 2243, "fields": { "state": 456, "issue": 57, "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19911, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 486, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": -0.06 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19912, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 460, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": -0.09 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19913, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 500, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": -0.12 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19914, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 484, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": -0.25 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19915, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 466, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": -0.13 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19916, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 496, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": 0.075 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19917, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 489, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": 0.045 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19918, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 481, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": 0.075 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19919, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 480, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": 0.04 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 19920, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "state": 490, "candidate": 153, "affected_candidate": 152, "state_multiplier": 0.02 } } ]; campaignTrail_temp.answer_feedback_json = [ { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1893, "fields": { "answer": 4413, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "And so it is. Susan Estrich is named Attorney General, and the Sasso-O’Donnell-Brountas triumvirate comes to dominate your inner circle during the opening act of your presidency." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1896, "fields": { "answer": 4418, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The Soviet Union is broke, and Gorbachev is looking for an off-ramp after years of investment in the Sandinistas with little to show for it. He accepts your deal, and the UNO coalition wins the election. Looks like Nicaragua’s long national nightmare is over." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1897, "fields": { "answer": 4419, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Mondale has spent some time talking up your negotiations with Gorbachev to the international community, and your decision to play it cool hurts your administration’s credibility abroad. Regardless, your position eventually wins out when the UNO beats the Sandinistas in the 1990 election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1898, "fields": { "answer": 4420, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Under fire from the Politburo as the Eastern Bloc falls apart, Gorbachev decides he hasn’t seen enough of you to stick his neck out like this. The Sandinistas lose the 1990 election anyways, but it’s no thanks to you, and allies abroad are starting to question whether you can execute your own agenda." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1899, "fields": { "answer": 4421, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Mondale has been likened to a “co-president” by inside observers, and he has already struck up a strong rapport with Gorbachev and even China’s new general secretary, Jiang Zemin. He works out a deal, and the Sandinistas lose the 1990 election. Leaders around the world note how little of a part you play in your own foreign policy." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1900, "fields": { "answer": 4422, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "In the best-case scenario, this complicates your attempt to win moderate voters. But you don't help matters by doubling down on this." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1901, "fields": { "answer": 4424, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This is probably the best you can do with this attack." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1904, "fields": { "answer": 4428, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Panicked Marines firing into the mobs. Soldiers being trampled and mauled by confused crowds. An Army helicopter clipping its tail on a water tower, crashing into the people below. These are among the snuff films broadcasted into ninety million living rooms, courtesy of Michael Stanley Dukakis." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1905, "fields": { "answer": 4430, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The few troops you send prove woefully inadequate, and Americans are glued to their televisions as Hollywood and Beverly Hills go up in flames. Willie Horton, the revolving door, your weakness — the Republicans were right about it all." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1906, "fields": { "answer": 4431, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Bentsen pauses at the mention of the elder Gore, lost in thought. That was nearly fifty years ago. He agrees with you that Gore is solid, especially compared to the scandalous Governor of Arkansas and his year-long shadow campaign for the vice presidency." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1907, "fields": { "answer": 4433, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Bentsen sighs and agrees to serve for a second term, adding that he wants to be more visible in a second Dukakis administration, even if he isn’t running for president in 1996. You agree and send him on his merry way." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1908, "fields": { "answer": 4434, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your stabilizing efforts are applauded by the foreign policy establishment, but fall mostly on deaf ears domestically. Surprisingly, you find Yeltsin to be a very nice man, even though he doesn’t really listen when you’re explaining to him your plans for a middle-class tax credit in your second term." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1909, "fields": { "answer": 4435, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Working in tandem with Mondale, you spend boatloads of capital getting Eastern Europe hooked on western aid, a huge boon to your image at just the moment you need it. Fortunately, the public pays less attention to the rise of far-right nationalist movements in opposition to foreign influence as democratic reformers lose all credibility." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1910, "fields": { "answer": 4436, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Mondale lights up like a kid in a candy store as you tell him your bold vision to shape the post-Soviet world. Your vision comes to a crashing halt as Congress blocks many of your proposals, wasting valuable political capital and making you seem more aloof than ever." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1914, "fields": { "answer": 4440, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This language could be a turnoff to some moderates, but your strength in holding to this position appeals to others." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1917, "fields": { "answer": 4444, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This is a gutsy move, but people get the message. In any case, at least they're looking at your face instead of a toxic waste dump." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1918, "fields": { "answer": 4445, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Many observers believe that you are too cavalier about the Republican attack machine." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1922, "fields": { "answer": 4450, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Press conference after press conference, the reporters hound Press Secretary Leslie Dach about Sasso, but she shrugs them off and keeps pressing on. Dick Gephardt won’t even speak to you during talks with House leadership." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1923, "fields": { "answer": 4451, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "In all fairness, political hardball like this is hardly unprecedented, but this was probably necessary anyways. You eventually rehire Sasso as an advisor in ‘91, though he never reaches the proximity to you he enjoyed as Chief of Staff." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1927, "fields": { "answer": 4457, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Bush's background is a bit of a liability. There's no reason to let him off the hook." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1928, "fields": { "answer": 4458, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As the months pass, the financial sector begins to calm down, and things are finally on the upswing again. But your low-profile regulatory tweaks make few headlines, and your role in stabilizing the financial sector goes largely uncredited." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1929, "fields": { "answer": 4459, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Word on the street is that Alan Greenspan is very happy with the direction of your administration on economic policy. Elsewhere, your cautious response gives many of your own supporters pause." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1933, "fields": { "answer": 4464, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Attacking Dan Quayle is a no-brainer. You can't let this terrible choice of running mate go unpunished." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1934, "fields": { "answer": 4465, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Attacking Dan Quayle is a no-brainer. You can't let this terrible choice of running mate go unpunished." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1944, "fields": { "answer": 4478, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As capital punishment becomes an issue, it's difficult to find a position that doesn't alienate some group of your potential voters." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1945, "fields": { "answer": 4479, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As capital punishment becomes an issue, it's difficult to find a position that doesn't alienate some group of your potential voters." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1946, "fields": { "answer": 4480, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Your new position is a bombshell, and its greatly unappealing to most Republicans." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1947, "fields": { "answer": 4480, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Unfortunately, this position alienates you with moderates in the current high-crime environment." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1958, "fields": { "answer": 4489, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "This is a mainstream position for a Republican to take." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1959, "fields": { "answer": 4489, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A naked support of free trade is taking things a little too far for the Democratic Party right now." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1960, "fields": { "answer": 4491, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "In general, your party supports liberalized trade -- unless you are really trying to push yourself over the top in a couple of states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1974, "fields": { "answer": 4501, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Trade negotiations like this take a while, and it’s unlikely that anything will be ready for congressional ratification until well after your re-election. But by entering negotiations so early and openly, you’re casting your lot very clearly here." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1985, "fields": { "answer": 4514, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "This is not the preferred answer for this situation." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1986, "fields": { "answer": 4515, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Your comments make the nightly news, and are somewhat controversial in the farm states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1987, "fields": { "answer": 4515, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This is not a popular answer in the farm states, and seems surprisingly glib on economic plight for a Democrat." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1988, "fields": { "answer": 4517, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "You seem to be admitting that the Reagan Administration made a big mistake here, and that you either contributed or were not involved in the big choices." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1989, "fields": { "answer": 4517, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This is probably your best response. Take the attack to Bush." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1990, "fields": { "answer": 4518, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1991, "fields": { "answer": 4518, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1992, "fields": { "answer": 4519, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1993, "fields": { "answer": 4519, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1994, "fields": { "answer": 4520, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1995, "fields": { "answer": 4520, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1996, "fields": { "answer": 4521, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1997, "fields": { "answer": 4521, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 1998, "fields": { "answer": 4523, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Maybe it’s not such a bad idea to style your plan off of something that, although broad in scale, carries the endorsement of groups like American Airlines and the American Hospital Association. Senate Democrats take your marching orders, and a congressional battle rises above the horizon." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2000, "fields": { "answer": 4524, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Summoning the Massachusetts brain trust to Capitol Hill, the team meticulously cobbles together a comprehensive plan from what you promised during the primaries, based heavily on your Boston plan. Off in the distance, the storm clouds of a congressional battle begin to form, dark and looming." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15495, "fields": { "answer": 4411, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A few of your closest advisors are spooked, but national Democrats are reportedly relieved to hear that you’re planning on taking this course. You spend the rest of your transition period going from delegation to delegation, meticulously adding senators and congressmen to your card file." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15496, "fields": { "answer": 4412, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You initially start out intent on keeping a carefully-arranged array of congressional leaders, national Democrats, and Massachusetts clique members in your orbit, but as Inauguration Day nears, the lattermost group ends up taking precedence over the previous two." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15548, "fields": { "answer": 15547, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15549, "fields": { "answer": 15547, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15565, "fields": { "answer": 15564, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15566, "fields": { "answer": 15564, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "fields": { "answer": 15662, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15664, "fields": { "answer": 15662, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15676, "fields": { "answer": 15675, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15677, "fields": { "answer": 15675, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15683, "fields": { "answer": 15682, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15684, "fields": { "answer": 15682, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Good luck tomorrow!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15743, "fields": { "answer": 4415, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Opening with a show of force, you sign an array of executive orders introducing a binding ethics code for civil servants and a system of performance metrics. The OMB doubles in size, lording over the Executive Branch with a watchful eye for fraud and abuse." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15745, "fields": { "answer": 4416, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You travel through the country, visiting areas worst-affected by the ongoing farm crisis. Farmland values are finally on the rise again, but as you speak with everyone from governors to local farmhands, it dawns on you how long it will take for the scars of this decade to heal for them." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15747, "fields": { "answer": 15746, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You meet a wholly ambivalent Maragret Thatcher at Westminster, promising America’s continued support for the transatlantic alliance. You’ve impressed European leaders with your proactiveness, but observers stateside are surprised by your lack of presence on the domestic front." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15748, "fields": { "answer": 4417, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Paul Sarbanes sends your meager plans over to Tom Harkin, who tacks on a set of sweeping provisions colloquially known as the “Americans with Disabilities Act.” By the time the Civil Rights Act of 1989 finds your desk, its scope goes far beyond anything you had imagined. You shamelessly take full credit for it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15751, "fields": { "answer": 15750, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "These piecemeal defense cuts will hardly land you a Profile in Courage award, but they pass after some back-and-forth ping ponging and you move on to your next legislative battle." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15755, "fields": { "answer": 15754, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Despite the first round of BRAC closures happening under Reagan, they’ve never been paired with aggressive personnel reductions like this. Republicans are quick to point to the military communities destroyed by base closures, and Congress declines to touch them. You come back later with an array of modest cuts to weapons programs, and they pass." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15756, "fields": { "answer": 4463, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "After some cajoling, you successfully get your requested cuts to the House floor, although your combined savings only end up being two and a half billion dollars. In protest of the MX missile cuts, Congressman Bob Dornan chains himself to a guided missile on display at Eglin Air Force Base." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15757, "fields": { "answer": 15752, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’ve aimed high, and Congress likes it. After some back-and-forth ping ponging between the chambers, your deep defense cuts become law, and you move on to your next legislative battle." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15759, "fields": { "answer": 15758, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Ignoring accusations of “creeping socialism,” you enact sweeping financial reform via executive fiat. Libertarians and conservatives scream bloody murder as you come back for round two, slamming shut tax loopholes to fund these programs. Won’t someone think of the millionaires and billionaires?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15762, "fields": { "answer": 15761, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You announce your plan via a prime-time televised address, pointing to computer-printed charts while you explain the minutiae of risky lending and deposit insurance. Does anybody know what the hell you’re talking about? No. But it must be pretty important." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15765, "fields": { "answer": 4502, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You maintain a back-channel with Salinas, but all significant progress is nonetheless stalled for the foreseeable future. On the bright side, this position keeps you in the good graces of many of the working-class voters who elected you in ‘88." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15772, "fields": { "answer": 15770, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Shock and awe as the technocrats come in. The White House org chart is cut to ribbons and rebuilt from the ground up as Bob Reich and Ira Jackson form the “Efficiency Review Task Force,” leading a total audit of federal departments. The public watches on as you print and sign dozens of executive orders, amazed at the pace of your reforms." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15775, "fields": { "answer": 15774, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You travel through the country, visiting areas worst-affected by the ongoing farm crisis. Farmland values are finally on the rise again, but as you speak with everyone from governors to local farmhands, it dawns on you how long it will take for the scars of this decade to heal for them." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15779, "fields": { "answer": 15778, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As the Massachusetts brain trust storms Capitol Hill, you field calls from congressional Democrats fretting about their intrusive presence. After some watering down in the Senate to clear the filibuster, the Civil Rights Act of 1989 is sent to your desk, and you sign it into law." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15782, "fields": { "answer": 15781, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You meet Maragret Thatcher at Westminster, promising America’s continued support for the transatlantic alliance. You’ve impressed European leaders with your proactiveness, but many observers stateside are surprised by your lack of presence on the domestic front." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15805, "fields": { "answer": 15804, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Southern and industrial-based congressmen balk, fighting tooth-and-nail to keep jobs in their district. Your cuts are themselves cut, and the final result is pitiful. You follow up with modest cuts to the topline defense appropriation, which are approved." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15817, "fields": { "answer": 15816, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "These piecemeal defense cuts will hardly land you a Profile in Courage award, but they pass after some back-and-forth ping ponging and you move on to your next legislative battle." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15821, "fields": { "answer": 15820, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’ve overplayed your hand. Congress is willing to cut military expenditures, but not by this much, and the final amount you settle for is significantly less than you had hoped. Looks like you’re going to have to scrounge together the money elsewhere." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15825, "fields": { "answer": 15824, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Despite the first round of BRAC closures happening under Reagan, they’ve never been paired with aggressive personnel reductions like this. Republicans are quick to point to the military communities destroyed by base closures, and Congress declines to touch them. You come back later with an array of modest cuts to weapons programs, and they pass." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15829, "fields": { "answer": 4525, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As your brain trust gets to work behind closed doors on a revised proposal, idea after idea is tacked on. “Regional Health Alliances” for insurers to compete in, a National Health Board to administer the program, and even an individual mandate all make their way into your ever-growing plan. A full-blown war for healthcare rises on the horizon." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15837, "fields": { "answer": 15836, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "In the face of a character assassination on you and your adopted bill, your rallies start to attract more hecklers than supporters. You abort your effort and rush back to Capitol Hill, but it’s too late. As anti-healthcare protestors picket congressional offices, the House gets cold feet, and the bill is tabled for the remainder of the 101st Congress." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15839, "fields": { "answer": 15838, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Byrd is willing to play ball, but he doesn’t work for free. Expanded Medicare reimbursement rates for Appalachia, funding for medical programs at WVU, and a new federal research center only scrape the surface of the pork. Negotiations stall, and you realize all too late that you may have put too much stock in Byrd as your silver bullet." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15841, "fields": { "answer": 15840, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your ambitious effort might end up being a little too ambitious. As you juggle a dozen senators at once, each one of them tries to tack on their own proposals, all mutually exclusive. Negotiations stall and sputter, and the trickle of minor bills that get through is nothing compared to the project you began with." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15843, "fields": { "answer": 15842, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Congressional Democrats are completely blindsided by your decision to shelve an effort you championed during the 1988 campaign. As Democrats on the Hill rebel in an effort to keep the Kennedy-Waxman effort alive, the united front for universal healthcare completely collapses." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15846, "fields": { "answer": 15845, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Outraged by your attempt to throw her under the bus during her race for a full term, Governor Evelyn Murphy holds a press conference calling you a liar. As unflattering stories about Democratic infighting dominate the headlines, support for healthcare reform flatlines, and Congress never takes it up for a vote." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15848, "fields": { "answer": 15847, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Angered by your cajoling, Richard Shelby uses a televised Oval Office event on rural job training to inform you, and all of America, that he’s switching parties and that your healthcare bill is toast. “Okay,” you shrug. “Anything else?”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15850, "fields": { "answer": 15849, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You steel yourself and go through the motions as the senators devour your proposal, pulling out anything even remotely objectionable to their anti-government, anti-spending sentiments. The final bill is clerked through Congress and signed into law, but it’s hardly recognizable compared to the ambitious project you began with." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15852, "fields": { "answer": 15851, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Although some of your reforms eventually do see the light of day as riders on unrelated bills, they get lost in the noise almost immediately. The only thing the public sees is that you announced a landmark bill and left it for dead, and that’s not an entirely inaccurate perception." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15855, "fields": { "answer": 15854, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You embark on a public tour to rally in support of the bill, only to be met with angry hecklers at every stop. As terrified letters pile up begging congressmen to stop the bill before it’s too late, support on the Hill collapses, and it never sees a vote. A week later, Mitchell announces the definitive death of healthcare reform in the 101st Congress." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15857, "fields": { "answer": 15856, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It winds up being very difficult, and you realize all too late that you may have pushed a little too far. After weeks of Mafia-esque harassment by Brountas and O’Donnell, the bottom falls out. A dozen blue dog Democrats link arms in opposition to the bill and a disgusted Richard Shelby switches parties, ringing the death knell of healthcare reform." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15859, "fields": { "answer": 15858, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You cut it loose to the Senate Budget Committee, gritting your teeth as the Senate parliamentarian tears it to pieces in a Byrd bath. The final bill is clerked through Congress and signed into law, but it’s hardly recognizable compared to the transformational bill you began with." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15861, "fields": { "answer": 15860, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "In perhaps the greatest act of political infanticide in recent memory, you butcher the bill behind closed doors, picking and choosing the best pieces to shove through as riders on unrelated bills. Your final piecemeal reforms are lost in the noise, and the public sees it as the ignoble act of cowardice it really is." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15877, "fields": { "answer": 15876, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You dot your i’s and cross your t’s, keeping the pressure until the moment of truth. The Health Security Act clears the House by three votes, the Senate sends it back with amendments, and the House approves the Senate version shortly after. You proudly sign it into law, leaving the fretting about just how to implement this monstrosity to your subordinates." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15894, "fields": { "answer": 15891, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Disregarding threats of outright mutiny, you press forward, peeling off Chafee, Jeffords, and Hatfield to clear the 60-vote threshold. The House narrowly approves the Senate version shortly thereafter, and you triumphantly sign the popularly-dubbed “Duke Care” into law. Not bad." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15896, "fields": { "answer": 15895, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You take to the negotiating table with every intention to defend your project. Breaux and Pryor are impressed by your gumption, and your bill passes narrowly over the filibuster despite staunch opposition from the Republican minority. You sign the popularly-dubbed “Duke Care” into law days after." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15900, "fields": { "answer": 15898, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Byrd is willing to play ball, but he doesn’t work for free. Expanded Medicare reimbursement rates for Appalachia, funding for medical programs at WVU, and a new research center only scrape the surface of the pork. You hold your nose, agree to it all, and the Basic Health Benefits for Americans Act is back on your desk before you know it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15903, "fields": { "answer": 15901, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The press mocks your glib “why can’t we be friends” attitude, but after a few weeks of negotiation, you emerge from the smoke-filled room with a mostly intact Basic Health Benefits for Americans Act and sixty Senate votes. An illustration in The New Republic depicts you with a pair of cutters, cutting through the barbed wire and trenches crisscrossing Capitol Hill." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15923, "fields": { "answer": 15922, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The backlash over your tax increases is swift and strong, but you’re able to contain the fallout and assert the importance of austerity. You also gain credibility for honesty, and moderates are reassured by your consistent deficit hawkery." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15925, "fields": { "answer": 15924, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Given that you haven’t added all that much in terms of discretionary spending, it’s not like you have a lot to gain by going against the grain of austerity. As deficits rise, your reckless spending spurs inflation and spooks investors." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15927, "fields": { "answer": 15926, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Bending over backwards to avoid tax hikes on the middle class, you successfully cajole Congress into holding off on the inevitable for one more year. But your stealthy tax hikes are noticed and called out relentlessly by conservative commentators, and the astronomical budget deficit projected this year spooks investors." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15931, "fields": { "answer": 15930, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The backlash over your tax increases is swift and strong. To make matters worse, your shiny new healthcare plan is badly damaged by sequestrations and budget cuts, and you’re barely able to avert a total gutting. On the upside, you do gain credibility for honesty." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15933, "fields": { "answer": 15932, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The growth “paying for itself” is a half-truth at best, and deficits begin to rise again. However, your shiny new healthcare plan avoids cuts thanks to your deft maneuvering, and the public largely views it as a shrewd defense of your own accomplishment. Business continues as usual." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15948, "fields": { "answer": 15947, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Ambitious, aren’t we? Unfortunately, you lack the political capital to pull this off, and your bill dies ignobly on the House floor amidst a coal-and-oil-state Democrat revolt. You have no time to reflect on your failures as you take to the campaign trail for downballot Democrats." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15950, "fields": { "answer": 15949, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As you sign the executive orders, your mind drifts back to Stelian. You’ve been thinking about him more lately. After the ceremony, you retreat to your study for the evening, finding solace in the still and quiet." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15952, "fields": { "answer": 15951, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Felix Rohatyn is appointed chairman of the NIC, and he spares no effort in providing you with all the white papers you’ll ever need. Whether everyday families will care about this as wages stagnate and grocery prices rise is another question entirely." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15956, "fields": { "answer": 15955, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your bill barely clears the House over a coal-and-oil-state Democrat revolt, and then meets significant headwinds in the Senate. However, you and Mitchell blitz it through before the opposition campaign can mobilize, and you sign the Clean Energy Futures Act into law soon after." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 15958, "fields": { "answer": 15957, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Surprisingly, the NIC accrues broad power over infrastructure policy, earning significant inter-agency clout. You, Reich, Ira Jackson, and Felix Rohatyn appear on the cover of Newsweek, bearing blueprints and hard hats, the West Wing in scaffolding behind you. The title reads, “The Handymen: Inside the Duke’s Executive Branch Overhaul.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16031, "fields": { "answer": 16030, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Ambitious, aren’t we? Unfortunately, you lack the political capital to pull this off, and your bill dies ignobly on the House floor amidst a coal and oil-state Democrat revolt. You have no time to reflect on your failures as you take to the campaign trail for downballot Democrats." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16033, "fields": { "answer": 16032, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Eager to do something, Bentsen establishes contact with Estrich at Justice, Lee at HHS, and Futrell at Education, attempting to create a comprehensive approach to drug prevention, treatment, and enforcement. His efforts are complicated by his low standing in the administration, given the past year and a half he’s spent sitting around." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16037, "fields": { "answer": 16036, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The Rural and Small-Town Development Act passes the House and Senate easily, and you hold a ceremony with farmers and small-state senators to sign it into law at the Oval Office. Hopefully all this pageantry makes people forget that the bill itself is a wholly unimpressive set of grants and loans, nothing more." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16039, "fields": { "answer": 16038, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your bill barely clears the House over a coal and oil-state Democrat revolt, and then meets significant headwinds in the Senate. However, you and Mitchell blitz it through before the opposition campaign can mobilize, and you sign the Clean Energy Futures Act into law soon after." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16041, "fields": { "answer": 16040, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Leveraging the insider connections he made during your national tour last year, Bentsen far exceeds your already ambitious plan, becoming a nationally-respected voice on drug policy. His policy recommendations are useful, and Congress sends you bill after bill." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16081, "fields": { "answer": 16080, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hopefully you can find your groove soon, because you’re in a divided government now. The Senate promptly expels David Duke, and the headlines read “DUKE vs. DUKE” as you travel down to Louisiana to campaign against him in the subsequent gubernatorial election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16083, "fields": { "answer": 16082, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "No matter how coolly you play it, voters know that the House flipping for the first time in nearly 40 years means something is going wrong for your administration. In other news, the Senate promptly expels David Duke, and the headlines read “DUKE vs. DUKE” as you travel down to Louisiana to campaign against him in the subsequent gubernatorial election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16085, "fields": { "answer": 16084, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hopefully you can find your groove soon, because you’re in a divided government now. The Senate immediately expels David Duke, and the headlines read “DUKE vs. DUKE” as you travel down to Louisiana to campaign against him in the subsequent gubernatorial election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16093, "fields": { "answer": 16092, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Democrats still retain control of Congress, so channeling your inner Harry Truman might not be your best bet here. As your calls to the Speaker’s office go straight to voicemail, you begin to sense that you may have made a mistake." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16095, "fields": { "answer": 16094, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As congressional leaders privately mock your glib attitude, a group of pranksters from the Speaker’s office set up a mock candlelight vigil to memorialize all the incumbents “killed” by your terrible midterm. You find the spectacle tasteless and disrespectful." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16097, "fields": { "answer": 16096, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You meet with Speaker Foley the next day to gauge House Democrats’ reaction to your speech. “They like your tone,” he shrugs, “but with all that word salad, they weren’t able to glean a thing about your priorities going forward.” Mission accomplished? Kind of?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16107, "fields": { "answer": 16106, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "An image of House Minority Leader Bob Michel shaking your hand makes the rounds in conservative, THRO-adjacent circles. Discontent simmers just below the surface of the House Republican caucus." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16109, "fields": { "answer": 16108, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "An image of House Minority Leader Bob Michel shaking your hand makes the rounds in conservative, THRO-adjacent circles. Discontent simmers just below the surface of the House Republican caucus." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16111, "fields": { "answer": 16110, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "An image of House Minority Leader Bob Michel shaking your hand makes the rounds in conservative, THRO-adjacent circles. Discontent simmers just below the surface of the House Republican caucus." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16120, "fields": { "answer": 16119, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’ve averted a schism in the party, but as gas prices soar, investors freak out, and horror stories come out of the Iraqi occupation of Kuwait, your decision not to take action becomes deeply controversial." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16122, "fields": { "answer": 16121, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You settle into the White House over the next few months, watching thermal footage of Iraqi soldiers and vehicles being torn to pieces by laser-guided munitions, the stealth bombers slipping away undetected. Progress goes smoothly." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16124, "fields": { "answer": 16123, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As gas prices soar and your own party recoils, you ram the authorization of force through the Senate on Republican votes. You watch from the Oval Office as soldiers board C-130s on the tarmac, as fighter jets fly over burning oil wells, the sky black with smoke. The war is over in a month." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16129, "fields": { "answer": 16128, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your troops are brought out of their stupor and to their feet as you make the case against the Republicans and their shenanigans. Dick Gephardt, leader of the House Democrats since Foley got the boot, tells you that you’ve torched any chance you had of earning their cooperation on any meaningful issue. “Can’t lose something you never had,” you respond sardonically." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16131, "fields": { "answer": 16130, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Executive orders become a lot less impressive when you remember that they can be undone with the stroke of a pen, and the average American is hardly excited by talk of regional development conferences. Your speech is forgettable and uninspiring, but gets the message across." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16133, "fields": { "answer": 16132, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "“The best America is yet to come” is not the kind of rhetoric you want to be espousing when you’re the sitting President. Why haven’t you brought the best America already? With your lack of legislative success over the past two years, your tired talking points ring hollow, and Americans find themselves bored listening to you prattle on." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16135, "fields": { "answer": 16134, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "In an unprecedented co-opting of Reaganite rhetoric, you stun progressive and conservative observers alike with your declaration that “the era of big government is over.” Hopefully this new position you’ve triangulated puts your presidency on safer ground." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16138, "fields": { "answer": 16137, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This comes with ups and downs. Congress does have an abysmal approval rating, and the GOP makes for a great punching bag, but they also control no levers of power on the federal level. That means everything that happens is on you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16140, "fields": { "answer": 16139, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your little bit at the beginning is awkward and inelegant, but you do successfully bait a few of the Republicans into clapping at your “doomed reelection campaign” before dropping the Reagan line. But the American people have gained relatively little insight on your plans for the future, and how you’re making life better for them." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16142, "fields": { "answer": 16141, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You succeed in riling up Republicans and taking an extremely forced, hollow victory lap, but everyday Americans are none the wiser on how you plan to make life better for them going forward." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16147, "fields": { "answer": 16146, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Deftly working alongside conservative Republicans, you engineer the bill’s failure in what many liberal insiders consider the dirtiest, most cynical move of your presidency so far. Regardless, you accomplish what you wanted, keeping the gun control debate off the headlines." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16149, "fields": { "answer": 16148, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Drawing on their own experience at the state level, the Massachusetts clique interfaces well with Willie Brown and Leo McCarthy, returning to Capitol Hill with briefcases full of policy papers. Unfortunately, they are met by pro-gun encampments on every corner, and lawmakers seem more than a little skittish about the prospect of a sweeping gun law." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16151, "fields": { "answer": 16150, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The amendment session leads to a clown show of different amendments that spirals out of House Democrats’ control. The final product eventually makes its way towards your desk, but it is so far removed from the original proposal that it doesn’t even share the same name." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16153, "fields": { "answer": 16152, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The public is less than moved by the Stockton survivors’ impassioned testimony. But they listen intently to horror stories of inner-city gangsters armed with assault rifles, and the NRA’s legions are eventually felled using hardline anti-crime rhetoric." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16227, "fields": { "answer": 16226, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your saving grace is that Bob Dole might be the only man in Washington more scared of the THRO revolt than you. The vote is smothered in the Senate, but not before Mondale becomes the first Cabinet member impeached by the House since the Civil War." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16229, "fields": { "answer": 16228, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Begrudgingly, you agree to turn a blind eye as Gingrich slashes the NEA and Education Department, and any mention of “delegate committees” vanishes from the headlines. Liberal commentators start to wonder aloud how much you’ll let him cut to cover your own ass." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16231, "fields": { "answer": 16230, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Nice try, but Hart is too petty–and too busy staving off Armageddon from his doghouse–to bail out Mondale. You fume watching him in front of committee the next month bemoaning his treatment by Fritz’s managers. This is going to be harder than you thought." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16233, "fields": { "answer": 16232, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The GOP Congress doesn’t need to explain the minutiae of campaign finance law. They point to a career politician getting more than his share, and the public sees it as a smoking gun. Mondale resigns before facing the ignominity of an impeachment vote, the only bright spot being that successor Lee Hamilton’s confirmation passes with unanimity." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16235, "fields": { "answer": 16234, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You have the upper hand for certain, but don’t fall into the trap of complacency. A good midterm doesn’t necessarily mean a successful presidency, and you don’t want to be blindsided by these people in 1992." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16237, "fields": { "answer": 16236, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your concern is probably warranted, despite the sunny outlook shared by the rest of the Massachusetts brain trust. God willing, Speaker Gingrich will be someone else’s mess, but you can’t afford to presume that." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16248, "fields": { "answer": 4432, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A satisfied Bentsen agrees, telling you that he doesn’t care who you pick as long as you win. Coming out of the meeting, you tell your team about the change to the ticket in the “Year of the Woman.” They quietly plan the running mate rollout, scheduled some time before the DNC." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16251, "fields": { "answer": 16250, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Before he leaves your office, Bentsen thanks you for including him in your administration and allotting him a high-profile role with the National Alliance Against Drugs. He needn’t remind you how the last Texan to hold the office was treated." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16270, "fields": { "answer": 16269, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Framed by the press as an innovator with Wall Street chops, Rohatyn is clerked through the Senate without issue. Initial volatility in the markets is short-lived, and the economy stabilizes significantly over the next several months." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16274, "fields": { "answer": 16273, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’ve won another victory for the technocracy, but a lot of your core base is frustrated with you. However, Greenspan does begin to aggressively cut interest rates, and the economy soon shows signs of life." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16276, "fields": { "answer": 16275, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Initially, your abandonment of Greenspan spooks the markets, and Rohatyn’s first few months as Fed chair see considerable instability. However, as the panic subsides, Rohatyn’s pro-growth policies will definitely become a boon to your re-election chances." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16278, "fields": { "answer": 16277, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Facing a flagging economy and low approval ratings, Rohatyn’s appointment is seen as a desperate gamble, and investors have a crisis in confidence. Bonds sell off, yields spike, and Rohatyn never gains legitimacy." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16282, "fields": { "answer": 16281, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You don’t have the credibility to pull this off. As markets tumble, Dole laughs your appointment out of the room, and you annnouce your reappointment of Greenspan a week later." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16284, "fields": { "answer": 16283, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You catch some criticism on the left for not putting up a fight against Greenspan, but given the Republican majority in the Senate, you’ve got to pick and choose your battles. Meanwhile, Greenspan begins to cut interest rates, and the economy soon shows signs of life." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16286, "fields": { "answer": 16285, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your bold gambit dominates the discussion, and Jim Jeffords breaks ranks to send Rohatyn through on a 50-49 vote. Initial volatility in the markets soon subsides, and Rohatyn’s pro-growth policies will provide you with a noticeable tailwind going into 1992." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16306, "fields": { "answer": 16305, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Ramsey Clark excoriates your flippant comment, calling you a war criminal and a traitor to the Democratic Party, highlighting the provision of the Geneva Convention that prohibits killing retreating soldiers. How anyone can feel that leve of sympathy for Saddam’s murderous goons is beyond you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16308, "fields": { "answer": 16307, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Although your intra-party clout as the sitting President is enough to win over a majority of Democrats, you’ve reopened old wounds in the party, falling on the side of the Cold War internationalists instead of the new generation of liberals who grew up in the shadow of Vietnam." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16310, "fields": { "answer": 16309, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The new generation of liberals, who grew up in the shadow of Vietnam, have been deeply disillusioned by your willingness to break your narrative of domestic renewal for the sake of foreign conquest. Your appeasement should be able to buy time with them until the election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16313, "fields": { "answer": 16312, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Expecting heavy resistance and civilian presence, an advance convoy of Marines enters Kuwait City in the dead of night. They find neither. Shapes and shadows warp across the bombed-out cityscape as the men advance by flarelight. The images are surreal. Dreamlike." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16315, "fields": { "answer": 16314, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "With little concrete leverage against the Iraqis, negotiations stall, and the final agreement is toothless. As Saddam’s troops continue to pillage a bombed-out Kuwait, you look weak on the national stage, played by the dictator of a Middle Eastern country most Americans don’t know the first thing about." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16320, "fields": { "answer": 16319, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Bush himself has no interest in running again, but he could play a considerable part in mobilizing opposition to your candidacy in foreign policy circles." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16322, "fields": { "answer": 16321, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Bush himself has no interest in running again, but he could play a considerable part in mobilizing opposition to your candidacy in foreign policy circles." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16327, "fields": { "answer": 16326, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s good optics to meet with trustworthy allies and potential future ones, and the whole process bolsters your credentials on foreign policy at home. Even so, you find it incredibly difficult to negotiate anything meaningful with the many different factions in the region. Exhausted, you take whatever you can get and return home." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16329, "fields": { "answer": 16328, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This delicate balancing act is noticed by the Emirati diplomats, but they tactfully choose not to push you too much on it, and the home front stays similarly quiet. The economic pie of the Gulf States will continue to grow, their reliance on oil will slowly begin to yield, and everyone stands to gain. You hope so, at least." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16331, "fields": { "answer": 16330, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’re welcomed into the growing urban landscape of Jeddah to meet King Fahd, locking up shiny new contracts regarding Aramco’s international presence and securing American companies a foothold in the Saudi state. At home, though, progressives lambast you, citing the Saudis’ abysmal human rights and environmental records." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16390, "fields": { "answer": 16389, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You cave, and the calamity that ensues is monumental. The last of the Great Society is gutted and destroyed, and your approval rating tumbles further as liberals abandon you in droves." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16392, "fields": { "answer": 16391, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Noticing your desperation, Dole slides you a deal identical to the one you just rejected, save for paltry concessions on food stamps and education funding, which you quickly accept. The finalized deal is met with shock and horror from your own party and passes through Congress almost solely on Republican votes." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16394, "fields": { "answer": 16393, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The shutdown drags through the holiday season. As poll after poll shows the American people blame the Republicans, Gingrich finally relents, and the government reopens. After years of frustration and marginalization, you’ve finally scored, as your approval rating stabilizes and even sees an uptick." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16399, "fields": { "answer": 16398, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Congressional leaders give you the thumbs-up on these programs with their requested budgets of 25 million and 250 million dollars, respectively. Unfortunately, the latter becomes deeply controversial as stories of high-profile scholarship fraud dominate the headlines." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16401, "fields": { "answer": 16400, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Bob Reich mobilizes the ERTF far beyond its original mandate, cajoling Congress until it delivers you a landmark bill restructuring the AFDC into a series of time-limited blocks. A little unnerved by Reich’s growing clout, you dissolve the ERTF soon after the bill’s signing as the House Oversight Committee subpoenas his entire office." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16403, "fields": { "answer": 16402, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Unexpectedly, congressional liberals rebel against your noble push for personal responsibility, and you end up shoving through a bill mutilating the AFDC while expanding job training programs, relying on Republican and conservative Democrat votes." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16405, "fields": { "answer": 16404, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "At your command, the Dukakis administration begins the long march towards welfare reform. You’ve stirred up controversy and conversation about the issue, but any substantive legislation on the matter won’t be ready until well after the election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16407, "fields": { "answer": 16406, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Over the next few weeks, the White House receives multiple bomb threats. In California, a death cult is disbanded by the FBI, frail ringleader Marshall Applewhite cuffed and slammed against an FBI van. The next day, a tearful Pat Buchanan, joined by William Cooper, decries your “unprecedented weaponization of the federal government.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16418, "fields": { "answer": 16417, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "These measures pass with little opposition, and Jesse Jackson is reportedly pleased to hear that you’re making steps on an issue that was so important to his candidacy in ‘88. Elsewhere, many consider your efforts too little and too late." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16420, "fields": { "answer": 16419, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Joseph Nye passes your plans to the Vice President, who has busied himself as drug czar. His prominence soars as he represents the United States at a hemispherical summit on drug policy, shaking hands with Presidents Salinas and Collor. If you aren’t careful, he might even outshine you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16451, "fields": { "answer": 16450, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your plan is lauded as visionary by the foreign policy establishment, but Gingrich immediately organizes a press conference, announcing his intention to block your “foreigners-first agenda” in Congress. As gangsters and dictators roam the Eastern Bloc and Central Asia with impunity, you find it difficult not to think about what could have been." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16453, "fields": { "answer": 16452, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Secretary of State Lee Hamilton does not hesitate to inform you that he thinks this is a mistake. The poor guy has yet to fill Mondale’s shoes; you can’t imagine the riot act Fritz would have given you if he were still here." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16455, "fields": { "answer": 16454, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Being caged like this drives Mondale insane, chomping at the bit to reshape the postwar order. During a meeting with NATO Ambassador Geraldine Ferraro, she lets slip that he floated resigning a couple of times." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16467, "fields": { "answer": 16465, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You aren’t exactly buddy-buddy with Gorbachev, but he has seen enough of you to take your word at face value. The finalized deal is hailed as a diplomatic triumph, and the UNO routs the Sandinistas in the 1990 election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16472, "fields": { "answer": 4429, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "On your orders, soldiers pour into the streets in helmets and flak jackets, setting up barbed-wire perimeters around Beverly Hills.
