They watch wide-eyed as smoke billows on the horizon." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16489, "fields": { "answer": 16488, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As Jackson barnstorms around the country, you stay planted in the Oval Office, confident that your Rose Garden strategy will take care of it. Let’s hope it does." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16491, "fields": { "answer": 16490, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "In a party scarred by two terms of Nixon, two terms of Reagan, and the disunity fostered by Kennedy’s primary challenge, your stern warning works wonders. Hopefully the Jackson issue resolves itself and you can focus on the external threat." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16504, "fields": { "answer": 16503, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "In the early campaign, these ads aren’t even explicitly tied to your candidacy, but they’re very important in influencing public opinion and keeping the focus on issues you have the upper hand on." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16508, "fields": { "answer": 16507, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The Rose Garden strategy is tried-and-true among incumbents, but these are unprecedented times, and Jack Corrigan in particular really thinks you could benefit from setting the narrative before the campaign." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16825, "fields": { "answer": 16824, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The pro-life activists don’t care. On the other side of the White House fence, they set up a nauseating demonstration, camping out in tents and waving images of mangled fetuses. If only they understood how little of a statistical effect abortion bans actually have on the number of abortions being performed." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16827, "fields": { "answer": 16826, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "On the other side of the White House fence, pro-life protestors set up a nauseating demonstration, camping out in tents and waving images of mangled fetuses. If only they understood how little of a statistical effect abortion bans actually have on the number of abortions being performed." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16852, "fields": { "answer": 16851, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The problem with scaremongering about Bob Dole is that he’s categorically not an extremist, even if he has been making overtures to the right during his campaign. Meanwhile, Dole gives the THRO movement the middle finger, and John DeCamp’s ranks swell with disaffected conservatives." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16874, "fields": { "answer": 16873, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’re hardly the most captivating speaker, but fighting for your agenda has shown voters what you stand for. With Dole, they can only tell what he stands against. Meanwhile, Dole gives the THRO movement the middle finger, and John DeCamp’s ranks swell with disaffected conservatives." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16890, "fields": { "answer": 16889, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s difficult to run as an outsider when you’re the sitting President of the United States, though your populist pitch migh carry appeal in certain areas. Meanwhile, Dole gives the THRO movement the middle finger, and John DeCamp’s ranks swell with disaffected conservatives." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16906, "fields": { "answer": 16905, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You smirk as Dole is accosted by conspiracy theorist hecklers at every campaign stop, calling him a “Benedict Arnold” for condemning anti-government militia movements. Of course, you’re harassed by the same groups, but it’s not as intense since they don’t expect any better from the Democrats." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16928, "fields": { "answer": 16927, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Lying on the Oval Office couch, your gaze meets that of John Quincy Adams, staring down at you from above. You turn away, frustrated and uncomfortable." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 16949, "fields": { "answer": 16948, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Buchanan’s stunning nomination has brought your candidacy back from the dead, but it only heightens the risk. There’s basically a 50-50 shot that either you get elected, or Pat Buchanan does." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17016, "fields": { "answer": 17015, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "On the balance, the public still thinks about the Reagan presidency favorably, but “trickle-down economics” as an abstract concept isn't nearly as popular. Meanwhile, many Robertson supporters declare Kemp another agent of the Freemasons and defect to third parties." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17040, "fields": { "answer": 17039, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "However little progress you have ended up making on a balanced budget, Kemp outdoes you in spades by openly not wanting one. Meanwhile, many Robertson supporters declare Kemp another agent of the Freemasons and defect to third party candidates." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17064, "fields": { "answer": 17063, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your record is probably good enough to warrant this kind of campaigning. Jack Corrigan and Nick Mitropolous present you with a revised general election strategy reminiscent of Truman’s reelection, portraying you as a decisive leader and Kemp as out-of-touch. Meanwhile, many Robertson supporters declare Kemp another agent of the Freemasons and defect to third party candidates." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17088, "fields": { "answer": 17087, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "In response to your jab at his former team’s recent back-to-back Super Bowl losses, he pokes at your Red Sox’s 74-year championship drought. As you play along with his stupid banter, a handful of Robertson supporters declare Kemp another agent of the Freemasons and defect to third party candidates." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17118, "fields": { "answer": 17117, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s a little juvenile, but Chris Edley is surprised to find that this talking point plays ridiculously well in your focus groups, better than any other attack. The ads are on the airwaves in a matter of days." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17131, "fields": { "answer": 17130, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As you watch Pat Robertson speak with disdain about the “pornography” you promote in the Oval Office by prominently displaying a print of Langolis’ Diana and Endymion, you muse about which is more concerning: Robertson indeed being a total fraud, or him actually believing the lunacy he spews." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17144, "fields": { "answer": 17143, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The “Jewish bankers” quote attracts controversy for making Robertson sound bigoted, but much to your chagrin, the general public seems shockingly indifferent to his Illuminati new world order insanity. What is going on?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17157, "fields": { "answer": 17156, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "John Sasso leads a campaign task force snooping around Robertson, highlighting all the odd characters who support him. Sovereign citizens, AM shortwave broadcasters, and apocalyptic end-time preachers all find themselves under national scrutiny as you leave no stone unturned." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17174, "fields": { "answer": 17173, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s a cheap attack, appealing to voters’ desire to be on the winning team, but with the wins you’ve racked up over the past four years, the bandwagon effect is undeniable. Meanwhile, POW/MIA militia leader and Populist Party candidate Bo Gritz burns Gramm in effigy, San Diego Stadium visible in the background." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17190, "fields": { "answer": 17189, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The Superconducting Super Collider of Waxahachie, Texas, which you support, is hardly the most important campaign issue, but it does put Gramm in an uncomfortable position to defend his vote. Meanwhile, POW/MIA militia leader and Populist Party candidate Bo Gritz burns Gramm in effigy, San Diego Stadium visible in the background." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17203, "fields": { "answer": 17202, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Jack Corrigan and Nicholas Mitropolous present you with a revised general election strategy reminiscent of Reagan’s reelection, sunny and positive. As you listen to them, newsmen cover a burning effigy of Gramm in downtown San Diego on the television behind them, courtesy of POW/MIA militia leader and Populist Party candidate Bo Gritz." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17234, "fields": { "answer": 17233, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your patronizing tone about Dornan’s health elicits another overreaction from him, and Jack Corrigan curses the Goldwater Rule under his breath as psychologists stay mum about Dornan’s mental fitness. Meanwhile, a young Dornan staffer named Randy Fine suffers a mental breakdown outside his congressional office in DC." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17248, "fields": { "answer": 17247, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The congressmen you speak to shock you with their candor, openly and smugly predicting a landslide defeat. Even so, the force of party loyalty is strong, and the public endorsements you net are miniscule compared to the private vitriol they have towards him." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17262, "fields": { "answer": 17261, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Sallie Dornan condemns and denies your attacks, admitting she was struggling with drug addiction when she made these claims. The media compares her to Kitty and her struggles with substance abuse, garnering great sympathy. You point to the jury conviction, but your continued attacks just come off as cruel." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17276, "fields": { "answer": 17275, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As you and Jack Corrigan go through a list of sunny talking points, your attention falls on the television behind him, where even Bob Dornan is being stalked by protesters calling him a COINTELPRO trojan horse. You look back at your clipboard. Something is deeply, deeply wrong with this country." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17292, "fields": { "answer": 17291, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s difficult to describe Newt’s appeal. He’s not charismatic in a traditional sense, yet sucks the oxygen out of every room. Regardless, almost nothing scares partisan Democrats like the words “President Gingrich,” and the party is ready to fight for you this November." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17308, "fields": { "answer": 17307, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Gingrich points out that Bentsen did the same thing in ‘88, you counter that senators are different since the governor instantly appoints a replacement senator. Voters are left wondering why this all matters and how it has to do with the economy, foreign policy, or any substantive issue." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17324, "fields": { "answer": 17323, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Gingrich counterattacks, claiming you’re misrepresenting your military service and that you served after the Korean War. You respond that you said “in Korea,” not “during the Korean War,” and that you were, in fact, stationed in Korea. The conversation is further muddled by the definition of “draft dodger,” but you still hold the upper hand." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17340, "fields": { "answer": 17339, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You reach out to a reluctant Jesse Jackson, who flips through his rolodex and points you to one of his foremost benefactors. Two days later, you’re seated before billionaire Donald Trump at his gaudy casino in Atlantic City. “Jesse sent you?” he asks. “Anything for him. I can work something out.” Just like that, the cash starts flowing again." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17367, "fields": { "answer": 17366, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As far as the American people are concerned, you have done neither. He offers fear and safety, you offer chaos and empty promises. The people of Kuwait know only the latter." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17385, "fields": { "answer": 17384, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Alexander can only do so much as Rummy’s domestic mouthpiece. Asked about Social Security on 60 Minutes, Rumsfeld stutters and tries to segue into Saudi Arabian security concerns." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17403, "fields": { "answer": 17402, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The effect here will be marginal at best. Buchanan despises Rummy but still considers you the grand architect of the homosexual Jewish new world order, who must be defeated at all costs. Whatever, man." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17421, "fields": { "answer": 17420, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You find yourself face to face with the former veep at his estate in Maine, where he candidly tells you that he’d have run himself to stop Rummy if he knew this would happen. You ask about the possibility of an endorsement, which he respectfully declines, citing the “dangerous precedent” you set by refusing to go to war in the Gulf." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17446, "fields": { "answer": 17445, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Leveraging the insider connections he made during your national tour last year, Bentsen far exceeds your already ambitious plan, becoming a nationally-respected voice on drug policy. His policy recommendations are useful, and Congress sends you bill after bill." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17450, "fields": { "answer": 17449, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Eager to do something, Bentsen establishes contact with Estrich at Justice, Lee at HHS, and Futrell at Education, attempting to create a comprehensive approach to drug prevention, treatment, and enforcement. His efforts are complicated by his low standing in the administration, given the past year and a half he’s spent sitting around." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17455, "fields": { "answer": 17454, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Enter feedback text here" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17459, "fields": { "answer": 17458, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Enter feedback text here" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17461, "fields": { "answer": 17460, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Enter feedback text here" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17464, "fields": { "answer": 17463, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A call for party unity is hardly revolutionary, but it might be the only card left up your sleeve at this point. The party claps along, and the wounded egos of the campaign season slowly begin to patch up and prepare for the general election. The convention bump registered by your internals is modest but very real, and it fills you with hope." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17466, "fields": { "answer": 17465, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s more or less the expected speech from the average incumbent seeking reelection, but you hit the right notes, and the American people watching at home begin to wonder about the possible—if only for a fleeting moment. The convention bump registered by your campaign internals is modest but very real." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17468, "fields": { "answer": 17467, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Railing against the bad guys is a tried-and-true method of ensuring party unity, even if it might limit your appeal down the road. Campaign internals register a modest but very real convention bump." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17482, "fields": { "answer": 17481, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Coasting your reputation off of Jack Kennedy is something you’ve done for years by this point. Even though you don’t have an iota of the charisma Kennedy had, your status as president does give you enough prestige to get away with talking like this." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17484, "fields": { "answer": 17483, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hope is a fickle thing, so fleeting and ephemeral. It matters not. Despite your own dour tone, despite the dismal state of the country, despite your own presidency, your impassioned declaration captures the imaginations of the audience for one more moment. Maybe the saints are coming after all?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17486, "fields": { "answer": 17485, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hope is a fickle thing, so fleeting and ephemeral. It matters not. Despite your own dour tone, despite the dismal state of the country, despite your own presidency, your impassioned declaration captures the imaginations of the audience for one more moment. Maybe the saints are coming after all?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17489, "fields": { "answer": 17488, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The eagle-eyed (or eared) viewer might pick up that your speech is nothing more than a loose collection of bumper sticker slogans, but it makes great soundbites for your campaign ads. The convention goes well, and the party is ready for battle this fall." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17491, "fields": { "answer": 17490, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hope is a fickle thing, so fleeting and ephemeral. It matters not. Despite your own dour tone, despite the dismal state of the country, despite your own presidency, your impassioned declaration captures the imaginations of the audience for one more moment. Maybe the saints are coming after all?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17493, "fields": { "answer": 17492, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Coasting your reputation off of Jack Kennedy is something you’ve done for years. Even though you don’t have an iota of the charisma Kennedy had, your status as president does give you enough prestige to get away with talking like this." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17496, "fields": { "answer": 17495, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hope is a fickle thing, so fleeting and ephemeral. It matters not. Despite your own dour tone, despite the dismal state of the country, despite your own presidency, your impassioned declaration captures the imaginations of the audience for one more moment. Maybe the saints are coming after all?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17498, "fields": { "answer": 17497, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s only been four years. You might be starting the rehabilitation of Reagan a little early. In any case, your speech is met with cheers and applause, although the odds of you securing mass Republican defections are slim." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17500, "fields": { "answer": 17499, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your words are simple, concise, and nothing to write home about, a far cry from the hype the DNC is trying to build around you in anticipation of your reelection. But, as you have known your entire life, humility is still a virtue." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17508, "fields": { "answer": 17507, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A call for party unity is hardly revolutionary, but it might be the only card left up your sleeve at this point. The party claps along, and the wounded egos of the campaign season slowly begin to patch up and prepare for the general election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17513, "fields": { "answer": 17512, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your speech is received warmly by the Democratic true and faithful. This party has long been kept afloat by “everybody else,” and the open class warfare waged by your predecessor has only solidified that. By uniting these various groups, you get a majority. At least, that’s the idea." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17518, "fields": { "answer": 17517, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s only been four years. You might be starting the rehabilitation of Reagan a little early. In any case, your speech is met with cheers and applause, although the odds of you securing mass Republican defections are slim." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17524, "fields": { "answer": 17523, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hope is a fickle thing, and unfortunately, you’ve let your people down one too many times. Your words ring hollow, and your convention bounce falls well below the historical average." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17589, "fields": { "answer": 17588, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your rallies go as well as you might have hoped, but your absence is noted, and {{opName}} gets significant coverage touring the disaster-impacted areas in your absence." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17591, "fields": { "answer": 17590, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You take to the Miami suburbs, wading through neighborhoods reduced to piles of twisted wreckage. In a matter that seems so trivial by comparison, the footage of you speaking to people in hard hats, pointing and gesturing authoritatively, makes for great ad material." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17593, "fields": { "answer": 17592, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You end up spending a couple of weeks trying to help in ways local leaders deem “a bit much,” and {{opName}}’s quick stops allow him to quickly get back to the campaign trail. Meanwhile, your absence on the national stage is noted by many." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17609, "fields": { "answer": 17608, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As you brave the rain and thunder to rally an audience in Lansing, Michigan, a lightning strike on a nearby transformer causes the power to go out. For a few moments, you’re just another man, shivering in the dark at a damp midwestern fairground. The power comes back on, and the moment is forgotten." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17611, "fields": { "answer": 17610, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The suburbs have long kept these states in the Republican column, but times are changing, and a rally with Chris Dodd ends up attracting more supporters than you anticipated. Will you be the Democrat who finally flips the suburbs this year?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17613, "fields": { "answer": 17612, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your off-the-cuff visits deep in the piney woods of Florida surprise and electrify supporters, many of whom cannot remember the last time they were visited by a sitting President. Within the Beltway, your visits are mocked, but you do meet real enthusiasm wherever you go." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17615, "fields": { "answer": 17614, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Embarrassingly, a brawl nearly breaks out between an organization of your supporters called “Rednecks for the Duke” and a group of Westboro Baptist Church lunatics picketing an event in Shreveport, Louisiana. Even more embarrassingly, the incident actually boosts your appeal in the southern states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17634, "fields": { "answer": 17633, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Bentsen takes his exile graciously and spends most of the campaign working to counter the influence of George Bush Jr., the man elected to his vacant Senate seat in ‘89. In the meantime, though, voters notice that the other half of the Dukakis/Bentsen ticket is missing in action." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17636, "fields": { "answer": 17635, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Poor Dan Quayle has become the scapegoat for Bush’s loss, although Iran-Contra probably did him in more. Either way, Bentsen makes for a solid, if uninspiring, surrogate for your campaign this year." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17638, "fields": { "answer": 17637, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Conflicts of interest be damned, Bentsen phones old friends back in Texas, getting the oil and banking money flowing into your coffers. They typically lean Republican, but they’re willing to bail you out because of your response to the oil crash back in ‘89." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17651, "fields": { "answer": 17650, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "There’s nothing wrong with making Al Gore the posterboy of your campaign - just hope nobody figures out that he’s barely more charismatic on the stump than you are." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17653, "fields": { "answer": 17652, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Nobody’s willing to say it out loud, but Al Gore’s recent tragedy with his son gives him a deeply personal perspective from which to think and talk about family issues." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17655, "fields": { "answer": 17654, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Gore is perfectly at home talking about the emerging Internet and the dangers of global warming, and young people can’t get enough of it. He becomes a favorite of college students with his lecture-like speeches about chlorofluorocarbons in the atmosphere." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17682, "fields": { "answer": 17681, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your advisors fret about the optics, but at her appearances around the country, the crowds begin to chant “I’m with her!” The energy is palpable." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17684, "fields": { "answer": 17683, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The Fall 1992 issue of Ms. magazine depicts a mock missing poster for Senator Barbara Mikulski. “Persons of interest,” the bottom reads: “Michael S. Dukakis.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17686, "fields": { "answer": 17685, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A “Dukakis/Mikulski” presidential ticket would cause a heart attack if a WASP from 1950 heard it, but times have changed, and ethnic Americans now enjoy more recognition and more acceptance than ever before. Mikulski is sure to help bring them into the fold." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17702, "fields": { "answer": 17701, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You jump back as gunfire riddles the podium and banner behind you. Realizing he missed, Marshall Applewhite turns the pistol on himself. You are ushered away, whisked back to the White House just in time for the results to start trickling in. Pat Robertson takes to the television to suggest the whole thing was a false flag." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17708, "fields": { "answer": 17707, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s like the flip of a switch. The nativism, the racism, the rank antisemitism didn’t get to them. The attacks on your mental state didn’t either. But this does, and Americans are nauseated by it. Buchanan’s numbers sink like a stone." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17714, "fields": { "answer": 17713, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Once the media gets wind of these insinuations, they are shocked… by Buchanan. As candidate after candidate disavows his rhetoric, it becomes clear that he’s finally gone a bridge too far." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17722, "fields": { "answer": 17721, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "News of your move to execute one inmate for political purposes sparks backlash and lawsuits from your own corner, particularly the ACLU, and some DOJ officers flatly refuse to carry out your order. Braving through the condemnation from all sides, you carry on in hopes that Middle America sees and understands." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17724, "fields": { "answer": 17723, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The death penalty will always be a liability for you in this environment, but this is probably the best way you can respond without contradicting yourself." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17726, "fields": { "answer": 17725, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’ve stuck to your values yet again on this issue. Your moral inflexibility might be admirable to some voters, but it’s definitely hurting you overall." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17728, "fields": { "answer": 17727, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The “elitism” spin and the Iran-Contra deflection is an interesting angle and starts a conversation about double standards. However, few forget your lack of a response to the actual issue of crime as it pertains to American cities, especially after the Los Angeles horrors." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17740, "fields": { "answer": 17739, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Many of your own supporters cringe at your use of the term “illegal aliens,” but Jack Corrigan’s focus groups indicate that this talking point plays well with working-class voters in crucial swing states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17742, "fields": { "answer": 17741, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Buchanan’s smug response: “I didn’t just mean illegal aliens.” You find yourself struggling not to loathe him." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17744, "fields": { "answer": 17743, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Buchanan’s concern is obviously not about white immigrants. Regardless, by emphasizing legal immigration, this kind of answer does get ethnic whites thinking about what Buchanan’s “immigration reduction” plans mean for people like them." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17746, "fields": { "answer": 17745, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "At best, most people have no idea what you’re saying. At worst, it seems to confirm many of the charges levied against you by Pat Buchanan. But your message is carried far beyond the reach of normal political rhetoric in certain regions of the country." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17772, "fields": { "answer": 17771, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Sasso shrugs it off as “cursory opposition research,” but obeys and buries the record. A few minutes later, you call O’Donnell and ask him to double the Nazi ad budget, just for good measure." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17774, "fields": { "answer": 17773, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hitting the papers the next day, it’s only another drop in the bucket of Buchanan scandals pertaining to Jews and the Nazis. Regardless, it should help your chances, even if not by much." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17784, "fields": { "answer": 17783, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Isn’t that kind of the point? McCain’s unforced error getting widespread attention and condemnation is exactly what your candidacy needs right now." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17786, "fields": { "answer": 17785, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Kemp maintains that he disavows McCain’s language and McCain himself makes a profuse formal apology, but for the first time, many voters, especially Asian-Americans, are really starting to question McCain’s judgement." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17788, "fields": { "answer": 17787, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your bold play for the Asian-American vote makes headlines, especially in Vietnamese-American communities, and your organizers on the ground are reporting an unprecedented groundswell of support for you in these areas." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17803, "fields": { "answer": 17802, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s bold of you to lean the other way instead of wearing your religion on your sleeve, but it definitely has appeal in certain areas." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17805, "fields": { "answer": 17804, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Framing Robertson’s attacks as an attempt to turn Christians against each other is a good angle that undercuts his core appeal, and your bold proclamation of faith resonates strongly in certain parts of the country. Just be careful about spending all this time on the defense." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17807, "fields": { "answer": 17806, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "When asked to comment about his armageddon predictions at his next campaign press conference, he deflects and backpedals, claiming he was “taken out of context.” He’s so smarmy and dishonest." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17809, "fields": { "answer": 17808, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The gloves are off. You watch from the front row as bereaved mothers, sisters, and husbands tearfully recount the last words of their loved ones before a national audience, a shocking and gruesome spectacle. You look up into the clouds and, for a brief moment, hope nobody is looking down at you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17823, "fields": { "answer": 17822, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "John sits back for a few seconds in silence. He nods and apologizes. You immediately forgive, telling him that this conversation never happened." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17825, "fields": { "answer": 17824, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The stories hit the newspaper the next morning. The fury directed at Robertson is astonishing, coming most of all from his own corner. You bury your own ambivalence and muse that this is the natural consequence of cultivating a moralistic base." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17834, "fields": { "answer": 17833, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Little sideshow or not, Gramm does have serious fundraising chops, if little else. Your aggressive campaign swing should help get the microphones out of his face." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17836, "fields": { "answer": 17835, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This has been the signature Democratic calling card for decades. Many core parts of the Democratic coalition still pull the lever for candidates like you despite being shockingly socially conservative because they know what’s in store for them if they don’t." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17838, "fields": { "answer": 17837, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Nice save, comparing Gramm to Reagan is giving him undue credit. Your campaign of high-budget positive ads are buoying your numbers nationally; fingers crossed that all this hullabaloo Gramm is making doesn’t change the big picture." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17840, "fields": { "answer": 17839, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This is an interesting angle, but it’s a feeble punch at best and dishonest at worst. Gramm’s voting record has always been ultra-conservative regardless of party affiliation, and jumping to the GOP in the post-Southern Strategy Texas is a very common political trajectory." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17855, "fields": { "answer": 17854, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "As soon as you call it “dirty,” a smile forms on John’s face, but he quickly suppresses it and apologizes. You tell him not to worry about it, but instruct him to destroy the documents. As he walks into the hallway, you remind him that this conversation never happened. He nods and vanishes." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17857, "fields": { "answer": 17856, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The fury from what is eventually dubbed the “Porno-Gramm” scandal exceeds beyond your wildest dreams. Gramm wholly denies it accuses you of leaking it to sabotage his campaign, but his credibility is toast. His polling numbers plummet." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17864, "fields": { "answer": 17863, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This is an ambitious gamble. Hopefully you can thread the needle and expand your coalition – and with it your mandate – to the fullest possible extent." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17866, "fields": { "answer": 17865, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Don’t count your chickens before they hatch. For every voter in this region who is indifferent to the issue, there is at least one who can be swayed by persuasive arguments about the rights of the unborn." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17868, "fields": { "answer": 17867, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "There’s no substitute for personally appearing in a region, but this is probably about as close as you can get. The ads are friendly and well-shot, and they’re definitely getting voters to pay closer attention to your ideas." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17870, "fields": { "answer": 17869, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You spend the next several weeks obsessing over the Appalachian region, even raising the specter of another regional development conference, much to the chagrin of Chris Edley. It’s definitely helping your numbers regionally, but for voters elsewhere, you’re nowhere to be found as the campaign heats up." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17918, "fields": { "answer": 17917, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Sasso obliges, and the matter is never brought up again. A teenage boy named Adam Laxalt will never know the decision you just came to, but thanks to you, he will live a normal life, actually going on to assault a police officer in a couple of years." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17920, "fields": { "answer": 17919, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hours after leaking the information, a reporter intercepts Laxalt and Domenici, blissfully unaware, walking out of the Senate chamber together. The next two minutes and 39 seconds are immediately immortalized as one of the most awkward moments in the history of politics." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17928, "fields": { "answer": 17927, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It doesn’t take long for ads to hit the airwaves highlighting Gingrich’s connections to people like Bill Cooper, Mark Koernke, and Jack McLamb by way of THRO circles. A frustrated Gingrich distances himself from these groups, although he still keeps up the attacks on you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17930, "fields": { "answer": 17929, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Despite you and Gingrich being worlds apart in terms of ethics and corruption, voters are so unimpressed by your record of leadership that it hardly matters to them either way. But you’re not wrong about Congress’s approval rating being well below yours." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17932, "fields": { "answer": 17931, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s true that THRO was never intended to be a partisan campaign weapon, and they don’t take marching orders from the GOP, but the overlap between Gingrich’s “burn it all down” attitude and their own makes them natural-ish bedfellows. Your returns here are marginal at best." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17944, "fields": { "answer": 17943, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Sasso nods and leaves. Over the next few weeks, you take a shameful amount of pleasure in watching a sweaty Gingrich deny and deflect the steady drip of humiliating leaks about his shady dealings, political scheming, and marital infidelity." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17946, "fields": { "answer": 17945, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Sasso agrees and apologizes, which you accept. You later end up using some of the less risky stuff, but Gingrich’s infidelity and the true extent of his shady dealings don’t see the light of day until well after the election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17957, "fields": { "answer": 17956, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The gears of war are turning. Every night the public sees images of Iraqi crimes in Kuwait; they want strength more than rationality. Rumsfeld seems to offer both." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17959, "fields": { "answer": 17958, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It's like Eisenhower never died. Rumsfeld begins shouting about domino theory and the snowballing despotism in the Arab world. Sadly, the people begin to buy it again." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17961, "fields": { "answer": 17960, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Rumsfeld pivots quickly, talking about how he can “stare them down.” Before long, people feel discomfort with Rummy shaking hands with a murderer." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17974, "fields": { "answer": 17973, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A popular liberal cartoonist depicts you as the Terminator, holding your arm out to a woman labeled “U.S. Economy.” “Come with me if you want to live,” says the speech bubble coming out of your mouth. Kirk O’Donnell pins it to the bulletin board in the West Wing break room." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17976, "fields": { "answer": 17975, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’re the President of the United States — you can land an interview with anyone on the planet. Relaxed and confident, you take to Good Morning America to explain how you engineered the ongoing economic upturn." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17982, "fields": { "answer": 17981, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A popular liberal cartoonist depicts you as the Terminator, holding your arm out to a woman labeled “U.S. Economy.” “Come with me if you want to live,” says the speech bubble coming out of your mouth. Kirk O’Donnell pins it to the bulletin board in the West Wing break room." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17985, "fields": { "answer": 17984, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’re the President of the United States — you can land an interview with anyone on the planet. Relaxed and confident, you take to Good Morning America to explain how you engineered the ongoing economic upturn." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17992, "fields": { "answer": 17991, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your campaign eventually decides to emphasize that a slight decrease in unemployment is a decrease nonetheless and a promising sign, but most people see through the spin." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 17994, "fields": { "answer": 17993, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’re the President of the United States — you can land an interview with anyone you please. You take to Good Morning America to label the numbers as indicative of a “slightly improving job market.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18000, "fields": { "answer": 17999, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "A conservative political cartoon circulates depicting you, holding a revolver, shooting a person labeled “ECONOMY.” Who finds this garbage funny?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18002, "fields": { "answer": 18001, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You take to Good Morning America to label the numbers as indicative of a “steady, healing job market,” pleading with the American people to “give it some time.” They can feel your desperation through the screen." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18018, "fields": { "answer": 18017, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Buchanan rattles off his prepared talking points, disavowing any support for Nazism and shifting the focus towards issues more pertinent to voters. But each time, his lines sound a little more canned, a little more tired, a little more threadbare." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18020, "fields": { "answer": 18019, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Buchanan fires back that you care more about foreigners than native-born Americans. You, in turn, ask him if non-native born Americans should be deported. He obfuscates, asking you why you support illegal immigrants stealing the jobs of American workers, you tell him not to dodge the question. Not a good look for Pat." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18022, "fields": { "answer": 18021, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’re not the only one who wants to check the drama at the door. Buchanan delivers a polished, restrained performance, and the two of you have a surprisingly civil debate on the issues. This is exactly what you’re looking for, and exactly what your supporters have dreaded." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18033, "fields": { "answer": 18032, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You begin to bait Dole during the debate, joking about his losses in ‘76 and ‘88. You eventually get a reaction out of him, but not the one you wanted. He turns to you. “Mr. President,” he intones, “stop talking about my electoral history like that. I don’t appreciate it.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18035, "fields": { "answer": 18034, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Dole mumbles something about the Laffer curve. You counter that the labor supply is too inelastic, asking “Who quits their job because their marginal tax rate went up three percent?” Boring, but not a bad point." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18037, "fields": { "answer": 18036, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Dole retorts that Campbell was against forced busing, and that you had the same position at the time. You respond that you didn’t fearmonger or mobilize a protest at the State Capitol, likening Campbell to George Wallace standing in the schoolhouse door." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18089, "fields": { "answer": 18088, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The ever-so-affable Kemp defuses your accusations, laughing them off to the crowd’s delight. He then launches into another football parable, but loses the audience when he compares corporate lobbying to a two-point conversion. Maybe you held him to a draw?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18091, "fields": { "answer": 18090, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Kemp shrugs off your snide comment with a laugh. But weeks later, as Robertson supporters start to shadow his campaign events, the LA Times runs a front page story detailing the surprising return of rumors about Kemp’s sexuality. Mission accomplished?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18093, "fields": { "answer": 18092, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "...you’re no Jack Kennedy.” The audience gives you a standing ovation. World hunger is solved. The environment is saved. Warlords and starving children join arms to celebrate your complete and total annihilation of Jack Kemp." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18178, "fields": { "answer": 18177, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Robertson completely misses your attempt to bait him into saying Orthodox Christians aren’t Christian and instead begins waxing about morality and the love of Christ again. You hope the audience at home finds him as grating as you do." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18180, "fields": { "answer": 18179, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Robertson is quick to claim that he has no intention of using the presidency to evangelize, but his objections are weak. It’s what he’s based his entire career on, including his campaigns, and you’re quick to point that out." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18182, "fields": { "answer": 18181, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Robertson counters your nitpicks with an impassioned declaration that your administration lacks soul, bringing the audience to its feet with an impassioned call for tax cuts and “pro-family policy.” You can’t believe people still fall for his charade." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18239, "fields": { "answer": 18238, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Gramm seizes on your 1981 comparison, hogging all credit for what he terms the “historic Gramm-Reagan tax cut.” You point out how he just made up that name, and that it’s actually called the Kemp-Roth Act, but it comes off to most like you’re just nitpicking." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18243, "fields": { "answer": 18242, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "An offended Gramm suggests you’re distorting his record to fearmonger. You ignore him and press on, likening his investment-based Social Security plan to a slot machine at a casino. It’s a very vivid image." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18247, "fields": { "answer": 18246, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Gramm explains that he wants to cut taxes across all income brackets, not just the wealthy. You’re quick to describe ruining the federal budget with tax cuts for the rich as “stealing our children’s future.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18296, "fields": { "answer": 18295, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Dornan’s face goes white as his own words leave your mouth. He stammers that he meant to say “Judas,” that that was the only quote in his life he’s ever lost sleep over. You follow up by asking him if he lost sleep over threatening to physically attack Congressman Tom Downey." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18300, "fields": { "answer": 18299, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Dornan is quick to assail your sunny recollection of the past four years, pointing to S&L failures and the ‘90 economic contraction. As you watch him run his mouth, all you can think about is when a Republican politician from Illinois snarkily told you that he hoped someone assassinated Dornan." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18304, "fields": { "answer": 18303, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your decision to bring foreign policy into what was supposed to be a debate on domestic policy is so brazen it even stuns moderator Jim Lehrer. But as Dornan fumbles for an answer, he definitely looks unprepared for the presidency." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18309, "fields": { "answer": 18308, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Rumsfeld is unprepared for a debate over Russia. Stumbling over his words, he mentions a “new world order” that they must submit to." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18313, "fields": { "answer": 18312, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This might be your best bet with the Kuwait crisis. Rumsfeld plays his usual line about strength, but this topic is stagnant." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18317, "fields": { "answer": 18316, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Rumsfeld quips back a line about you being more concerned with your image than the safety of the American people. Such rare energy for such a bland man." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18322, "fields": { "answer": 18321, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "His practiced smile breaks into crocodile tears. “I cannot believe that the sitting President is so willing to demean his position with this kind of rhetoric,” he moans. He spends the rest of the debate gibbering about the press accepting this bad behavior. America is less than convinced, though it falls short of the knockout punch you might have hoped for." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18326, "fields": { "answer": 18325, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your base shrieks in horror as you sit with the man who has destroyed your government and play nice. You feel yourself shrinking, all attention drawn to the awful animal beside you. Any attempt at policy gets lost in the noise as Gingrich twists the knife. “Sure, he says all the liberal talking points,” Gingrich muses. “But if you see his record, he’s signing our bills. I think even he might know which side has it right.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18330, "fields": { "answer": 18329, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Gingrich responds with all kinds of attacks, on the failures of your administration, on your smearing of the Republican party. Ultimately, your dual partisan drives largely come to a draw. His blistering defense does not go as far as David Duke, whose mention forces Gingrich to cough out a statement denouncing “race-baiting.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18433, "fields": { "answer": 18432, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The last two years of Democratic frustration in the House have provided you dozens of alleged ethical violations, many of them true, and a few of them damning. But Gingrich collapses all of it in one quip. “Mr. President, you have passed my budget and signed my bills into law. Newt Gingrich is governing America already.”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 18440, "fields": { "answer": 18439, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Rumsfeld chuckles and asks rhetorically for Saddam’s feelings on it. You hear a few giggles in the live audience." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19086, "fields": { "answer": 19085, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Pat Buchanan once again disavows white supremacy, insisting that his campaigning for Duke was solely because Duke’s expulsion for simply having a different opinion was “anti-democratic” and “anti-free speech.” It’s a terrible excuse, and voters know it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19170, "fields": { "answer": 19169, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The economic populism aspect is a clever subversion of expectations, but you might not want to compare him to mainstream Republicans. With the current state of the country, that’s just what they’d prefer right now over either you or Buchanan." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19642, "fields": { "answer": 19641, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Short, humble, and to the point. You’ve never been one to self-aggrandize or steal the spotlight." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19644, "fields": { "answer": 19643, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Short, humble, and to the point. You’ve never been one to self-aggrandize or steal the spotlight." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19647, "fields": { "answer": 19646, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "It’s only been four years. You might be starting the rehabilitation of Reagan a little early. In any case, your speech is met with cheers and applause, although the odds of you securing mass Republican defections are slim." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19651, "fields": { "answer": 19650, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Your speech is received warmly by the Democratic true and faithful. This party has long been kept afloat by “everybody else,” and the open class warfare waged by your predecessor has only solidified that. By uniting these various groups, you get a majority. At least, that’s the idea." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19661, "fields": { "answer": 19660, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Hope is a fickle thing, so fleeting and ephemeral. It matters not. Despite your own dour tone, despite the dismal state of the country, despite your own presidency, your impassioned declaration captures the imaginations of the audience for one more moment. Maybe the saints are coming after all?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19677, "fields": { "answer": 19676, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You’re by no means an inspiring speaker, but this is probably where you’re at your best. Talk seriously, talk about the issues, and don’t make an ass of yourself." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19716, "fields": { "answer": 19715, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Eager to redeem himself after last March, Sasso leads Reich and Brountas deep into the swamps of Washington, negotiating tirelessly until a compromise bill eventually finds your desk. Although modest, relentless right-wing scaremongering has greatly influenced the public’s perception of this bill, and they readily believe you’ve really gone the distance. Your approval rating ticks up as you sign “Duke Care” into law." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19721, "fields": { "answer": 19720, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Eager to redeem himself after last March, John Sasso leads Reich and Brountas deep into the swamps of Washington, negotiating and pressuring tirelessly until wide swaths of the Health Security Act make their way into what was originally a revenue bill. Lloyd Bentsen casts the tie-breaking vote in the Senate, and you sign it into law soon after." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19725, "fields": { "answer": 19724, "candidate": 152, "answer_feedback": "As the whole debate devolves into pointless mudslinging over the partisan affiliations of the Keating Five, Wendell Ford and Harry Reid pull you aside and ask that you try a different line of attack. “Are you serious?” you fume. “Why don’t you guys try not being corrupt?”" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19727, "fields": { "answer": 19726, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "King disputes your assertions on both of these records, stating that labor unions and lobbyists sabotaged his tax rollback. Once more, the clock is turned back ten years, and voters are starting to get a little sick of the “political feud” narrative." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19729, "fields": { "answer": 19728, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Boxed into something of a corner, King agrees. It takes a while for him to mentally paper over half of his talking points, and you use that lag time to great effect, explaining in detail your plan for job growth." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19731, "fields": { "answer": 19730, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "King has prepared a list of mental talking points on foreign policy issues, but they don’t survive much scrutiny. He resorts to accusing you of relying on Secretary Mondale to do your foreign policy for you, a valid but feeble punch." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19736, "fields": { "answer": 19735, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "An outraged King brings up John Coady, the man responsible for hiring Barczak, who committed suicide after the scandal broke out. His response completely backfires, as most voters consider Coady’s suicide a sure sign of guilt. You stop pressing the attack out of disgust with yourself, King, and the whole country." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19738, "fields": { "answer": 19737, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The physical strain on your body is immense, and you shrug off warnings from your personal doctor about the dangers of overexertion. Meanwhile, poor old King seems more and more exhausted at every campaign stop, and he’s eventually forced to cut back on his schedule." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19740, "fields": { "answer": 19739, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "This was a major embarrassment for King when it first leaked in 1979, but it’s lost its luster since then. Worse still, it creates an image of King as this jolly “Old King Cole” type, hardly concerning or threatening. Is your best attack that he ate lobster as governor ten years ago?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19742, "fields": { "answer": 19741, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "You cut a dark, stylized ad depicting an ominous man stepping out of a Lincoln. He ascends the steps of the Capitol, flanked by officers with batons and motorcycle helmets. But all the voters see is order and strength. Chris Edley scrambles to pull the ad from the airwaves as King’s ad men recut and replay it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19745, "fields": { "answer": 19744, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "And straight into the mud it goes. It’s like the past decade never happened as King brings up scandals from nearly twenty years ago, you debunking them all with methodical precision." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19747, "fields": { "answer": 19746, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Gimmick or not, you can’t hide your disappointment that your last frontier is yet another election against Ed King. The media is quick to dub the campaign as “a decade in the making,” and it’s off to the races against your arch-nemesis." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19749, "fields": { "answer": 19748, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "King’s status as a former Democratic governor gives him more crossover appeal than someone with his record and positions probably should have. Regardless of party, he’s always been a staunch conservative, and it’s hardly a bad idea to skip over all the personal drama and chip away at that image of his." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19751, "fields": { "answer": 19750, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "King tours the country, mocking the “ridiculous and egotistical notion” that you “single-handedly transformed an entire state,” portraying himself as a humble everyman all the while. This is going to be more frustrating than you thought." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19860, "fields": { "answer": 19859, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Campaigning early may be prudent for a man lesser than you, but with the credibility you’ve managed to build up, seeing you on television every day behind a presidential podium is all the advertising you’ll need. At least for now." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19863, "fields": { "answer": 19862, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The battle begins between you and Newt, but it doesn’t yield the results you’d hoped for. Newt uses the free press to bash your agenda relentlessly, and it drags you down to his level." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19866, "fields": { "answer": 19865, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Dole readily agrees that Newt’s deplorable antics cheapen the office and make a spectacle out of a serious process. But he rejects your ambitious efforts for an informal coalition, claiming that Newt doesn’t have the leverage to back his threat." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19871, "fields": { "answer": 19870, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Normally, a visit to a disaster site as cursory as yours might draw controversy, but Bentsen’s presence on the ticket both helps him bring relief to the region and earns the ticket positive publicity. You keep up the campaign nationally, and the Dukakis/Bentsen ticket is working at its best." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19880, "fields": { "answer": 19879, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "The Stalin comparison holds little water in a post-Cold War America, and the Mussolini comparison doesn’t fare much better. But you find success in a more abstract ad depicting his face on a black-and-white telescreen, mouthing propaganda to brainwashed silhouettes below." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 19934, "fields": { "answer": 19933, "candidate": 153, "answer_feedback": "Surprisingly, the NIC accrues broad power over infrastructure policy, earning significant inter-agency clout. You, Reich, Ira Jackson, and Felix Rohatyn appear on the cover of Newsweek, bearing blueprints and hard hats, the West Wing in scaffolding behind you. The title reads, “The Handymen: Inside the Duke’s Executive Branch Overhaul.”" } } ]; campaignTrail_temp.candidate_image_url = "https://itsastronomical.com/assets/1992Duke/imgur/DBEY7yF.png"; campaignTrail_temp.running_mate_image_url = "https://itsastronomical.com/assets/1992Duke/imgur/F1P3Ha2.png"; campaignTrail_temp.candidate_last_name = "Dukakis"; campaignTrail_temp.running_mate_last_name = "Bensten"; campaignTrail_temp.jet_data = [{ "banner_data": { "canName": "Dukakis", "canImage": "https://itsastronomical.com/assets/1992Duke/imgur/DBEY7yF.png", "runName": "Bensten", "runImage": "https://itsastronomical.com/assets/1992Duke/imgur/F1P3Ha2.png" }, "banner_enabled": true, "nicknames": { "152": "GOP", "153": "Duke", "154": "Lib", "155": "Green" }, "mapping_data": {}, "cyoa_variables": {}, "player_candidate": 153 } ] e = campaignTrail_temp; e.multiple_endings = true; campaignTrail_temp.cyoa = true ans = campaignTrail_temp.player_answers[campaignTrail_temp.player_answers.length - 1] campaignTrail_temp.player_answers = []; campaignTrail_temp.collect_results = true; function ctsAchievement(achievement, difficultyChecker = true){ if ((difficultyChecker && campaignTrail_temp.difficulty_level_multiplier<=1)||!difficultyChecker) if(campaignTrail_temp.CTS){ unlockAchievement(achievement); } } // Variables // Initiatives: The small victories. Minor laws, executive orders, things of that matter. var wins = 0; // Major successes: The type of achievements that define your legacy. var victories = 0; // Orbit: Who are you surrounding yourself with, congressional leaders and national Democrats or your insular Massachusetts clique? var orbit = 0; // Governing philosophy: Are you governing as a traditional Democrat, or are you governing more like a New Democrat? var govern = 0; // Economy: What is the state of the economy right now? How are people feeling about the economy? var econ = 0; // Aura: Are you projecting strength? Do people have faith in your leadership? var aura = 0; // Used to calculate your opponents - victories count as 1, wins count as 0.33. Gives the player more room to maneuver. const getTotalScore = () => victories + wins / 3; // Saddam + Angry Base Variable var saddam = 0; var angryBase = 0; // Tracks various requirements for certain events var firedSasso = false; var foreignVisit = false; var nafta = false; var gorby = false; var reich = false; var regDev = false; var byrd = false; var wonMidterms = false; var congress = true; var lloydDrug = false; var fritzRemoved = false; var theyHatedYouForSpeakingTheTruth = false; var heavensGate = false; var bibleQuote = false; var assaultBan = false; var sassoRatfucks = false; var mondaleDeal = false; var congressionalAction = false; var saudiVisit = 0; // 0 for no visits, 1 for visiting var fedPoint = 0; // 0 for Greenspan, 1 for Rohatyn var healthPoint = 0; // 0 for fail, 1 for success var vp = "lloyd"; // Can be "lloyd", "gore", or "mikulski" window.opName = 'TBD'; var dnc = ""; var ertf = false; var ertfReal = false; var russiaDeal = false; var triangulateSOTU = false; // Response to Iraq invasion of Kuwait var noResponse = false; var bombing = false; var coalition = false; // Healthcare bill // 1 is Kennedy-Waxman, 2 is MA plan, 3 is Clintonite var healthcare = 0; var healthcarePassed = false; // Variables to track how close you are to getting certain candidates var newt = 0; var rummy = 0; var king = 0; if (window.introSheet && document.head.contains(window.introSheet)) { document.head.removeChild(window.introSheet); } const customStyling = document.createElement("style"); customStyling.innerHTML = ` #campaign_sign { background-image: url("https://itsastronomical.com/assets/1992Duke/imgur/MGeWOe1.png"); background-size: cover; border-color: #c9c9c9; border-width: .01em; margin: 0 auto; background-position: -360px; width: 100%; height: 104px; margin-left: -0.07em; } #campaign_sign p { color: rgb(0 0 0 / 0%); } .caption-container { opacity: 1; transition-delay: 0.5s; border-bottom: 1px dotted darkblue; } `; document.head.appendChild(customStyling); cyoAdventure = function (a) { let pop_vote = e.current_results[0]; // 0.2 = 20% let dukePoll = (pop_vote.find(p => p.pk === 153)).pvp let repPoll = (pop_vote.find(p => p.pk === 152)).pvp let thirdPoll = (pop_vote.find(p => p.pk === 154)).pvp ans = campaignTrail_temp.player_answers[campaignTrail_temp.player_answers.length - 1] if (campaignTrail_temp.question_number == 0) { const customStyling = document.createElement("style"); customStyling.innerHTML = ` #campaign_sign { background-image: url("https://itsastronomical.com/assets/1992Duke/imgur/zsaEFNW.png"); background-size: cover; border-color: #c9c9c9; border-width: .01em; margin: 0 auto; background-position: center; width: 100%; height: 104px; margin-left: -0.07em; } #campaign_sign p { color: rgb(0 0 0 / 0%); }`; document.head.appendChild(customStyling); console.log(getOpponentDeck()); } // Variables // Initiatives if (ans == 4416 || ans == 15774 || ans == 15746 || ans == 15750 || ans == 15816 || ans == 15774 || ans == 15758 || ans == 15761 || ans == 4418 || ans == 4421 || ans == 15778 || ans == 4415 || ans == 15781 || ans == 4463 || ans == 15752 || ans == 15849 || ans == 15858 || ans == 15860 || ans == 15949 || ans == 16036 || ans == 16150 || ans == 16285 || ans == 16275 || ans == 16328 || ans == 16330 || ans == 16398 || ans == 16406 || ans == 16417 || ans == 16404 || ans == 4434 || ans == 4436) { // +1 wins += 1; } // Victories/Major Successes if (ans == 15770 || ans == 4417 || ans == 15898 || ans == 15901 || ans == 15891 || ans == 15895 || ans == 15876 || ans == 15957 || ans == 19933 || ans == 15955 || ans == 16040 || ans == 17445 || ans == 16106 || ans == 16108 || ans == 16110 || ans == 16152 || ans == 16269 || ans == 16419 || ans == 16402 || ans == 16400 || ans == 4435 || ans == 16465 || ans == 16123 || ans == 16038 || ans == 19715 || ans == 19720) { // +1 victories += 1; } // Orbit if (ans == 4411) { // +2 orbit += 2; } if (ans == 15746 || ans == 4451 || ans == 15778 || ans == 15781 || ans == 16096 || ans == 16402 || ans == 19865) { // +1 orbit += 1; } if (ans == 4412 || ans == 4450 || ans == 4458 || ans == 15770 || ans == 16080 || ans == 16092 || ans == 16128 || ans == 16400 || ans == 19862) { // -1 orbit -= 1; } if (ans == 4413) { // -2 orbit -= 2; } // Governing Philosophy if (ans == 4416 || ans == 15774 || ans == 15758 || ans == 4523 || ans == 4502 || ans == 15924 || ans == 15932 || ans == 15955 || ans == 16036 || ans == 16285 || ans == 16275 || ans == 16269 || ans == 16398) { // +1 govern += 1; } if (ans == 4459 || ans == 15761 || ans == 4524 || ans == 4501 || ans == 15922 || ans == 15930 || ans == 16042 || ans == 16273 || ans == 16404 || ans == 16402 || ans == 16400) { // -1 govern -= 1; } if (ans == 16084) { govern -= 2; } // Economy if (ans == 16269 || ans == 1) { // +2 econ += 2; } if (ans == 16285 || ans == 16283 || ans == 16273 || ans == 16275) { // +1 econ += 1; } if (ans == 15922 || ans == 15932 || ans == 16119 || ans == 16121 || ans == 16123 || ans == 16277) { // -1 econ -= 1; } if (ans == 15924 || ans == 15926 || ans == 15930) { // -2 econ -= 2; } // Aura if (ans == 4416 || ans == 15774 || ans == 15746 || ans == 15770 || ans == 15781 || ans == 15898 || ans == 15901 || ans == 15891 || ans == 15895 || ans == 15876 || ans == 15922 || ans == 15957 || ans == 19933 || ans == 16106 || ans == 16108 || ans == 16110 || ans == 16123 || ans == 16128 || ans == 16134 || ans == 16137 || ans == 16139 || ans == 16285 || ans == 16326 || ans == 4428 || ans == 4430 || ans == 19715 || ans == 19720 || ans == 15761 || ans == 4415 || ans == 15748 || ans == 16393) { // +1 aura += 1; } if (ans == 15926 || ans == 3 || ans == 15860 || ans == 16119 || ans == 16132 || ans == 16314) { // -1 aura -= 1; } // Saddam Variable if (ans == 15746 || ans == 15781) { // +1 saddam += 1; } if (ans == 15754 || ans == 15824 || ans == 4419 || ans == 4421 || ans == 15752) { // -1 saddam -= 1; } // Angry Base Variable if (ans == 16389 || ans == 16391) { // +2 angryBase += 2; } if (ans == 16134 || ans == 16123 || ans == 16146 || ans == 16228 || ans == 16305 || ans == 16312 || ans == 16330 || ans == 16404 || ans == 16402 || ans == 16400) { // +1 angryBase += 1; } if (ans == 16106 || ans == 16108 || ans == 16110) { // -1 angryBase -= 1; } // Checks for certain access to future answers if (ans == 15770) { ertf = true; ctsAchievement("Robert Reich DOGE"); king += 1; } if (ans == 16400) { ertfReal = true; } if (ans == 4451) { firedSasso = true; } if (ans == 15746 || ans == 15781) { foreignVisit = true; } if (ans == 16465) { gorby = true; } if (ans == 15770){ reich = true; } if (ans == 15898 || ans == 15901 || ans == 15891 || ans == 15895 || ans == 15876 || ans == 19715 || ans == 19720) { healthcarePassed = true; ctsAchievement("Ahab's Revenge"); healthPoint += 1 } if (ans == 4416 || ans == 15774){ regDev = true; } if (ans == 15898 || ans == 15838) { byrd = true; } if (ans == 16040 || ans == 17445){ lloydDrug = true; } if (ans == 16232){ fritzRemoved = true; ctsAchievement("Goodbye, Sweet Prince"); } if (ans == 4501) { nafta = true; } if (ans == 16927 || ans == 16990) { theyHatedYouForSpeakingTheTruth = true; } if (ans == 16406) { heavensGate = true; } if (ans == 16152) { assaultBan = true; } if (ans == 17156) { bibleQuote = true; } if (ans == 16330) { saudiVisit += 1; } if (ans == 16465) { mondaleDeal = true; } if (ans == 4435) { russiaDeal = true; } if (ans == 16134) { triangulateSOTU = true; } if (ans == 4417 || ans == 15901 || ans == 15898 || ans == 15891 || ans == 15895 || ans == 19715 || ans == 15876 || ans == 19720 || ans == 15955 || ans == 16038) { congressionalAction = true; } if (ans == 16419) { ctsAchievement("You're No Lloyd Bentsen"); } if (ans == 16123 || ans == 16312) { ctsAchievement("Mosul Miracle"); } // Checks for Newt Gingrich and Donald Rumsfeld's prerequisites if (ans == 16228 || ans == 16389 || ans == 16391) { // Newt +1 newt += 1; } // Checks for the conditions for Ed King if (ans == 15930 || ans == 15922 || ans == 16226 || ans == 16393) { king += 1; } // Checks for fedPoint if (ans == 16269 || ans == 16275 || ans == 16285) { fedPoint += 1; } // Checkpoints for new versions of each question if (campaignTrail_temp.question_number == 0 && orbit < 0) { campaignTrail_temp.questions_json[1] = { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15769, "fields": { "priority": 1, "description": "It’s been less than a week since you put your hand on the Bible, and your first hundred days as President are already in danger of being overshadowed. As Speaker Jim Wright founders under the weight of an ethics investigation over financial misconduct, Democrats are eager to cast him overboard and get moving with your agenda, with the ultimate goal of healthcare reform sometime next year. But that’s still a long way out, and you need to take the small steps first. Fortunately, the Executive Branch is not implicated in this scandal, so you’ve got a bit of leeway here.", "likelihood": 1 } } } if (campaignTrail_temp.question_number == 1 && orbit < 1) { campaignTrail_temp.questions_json[2] = { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15803, "fields": { "priority": 1, "description": "Over the past decade, defense spending ($303 billion annually as of 1988) has skyrocketed, soaring from about 5 percent of the United States’ total GDP under Carter to nearly 7 percent in some of Reagan’s budgets. From the outset, you’ve vowed to pass defense cuts, both to trim the deficit and fund your own domestic programs. Foley and Mitchell are willing to play ball, but you’re facing headwinds from congressmen wary of losing jobs in their districts. How will you approach this?", "likelihood": 1 } } } // Checks your healthcare bill choice for variable tracking if (ans == 4523) { healthcare = 1; campaignTrail_temp.questions_json[8] = { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15835, "fields": { "priority": 1, "description": "As the healthcare fight takes shape, it’s hard not to be disappointed by the ancillary role you’ve played in shaping the debate. Instead of taking your visionary Massachusetts system to the national stage, it’s just become another battle between Democrats and Republicans, the exact type of vicious gridlock you hate. Even so, you’ve expended vast amounts of capital shepherding it through the House over bitter opposition from Republicans and health insurance executives wary of “socialized medicine,” and now the fate of your keystone initiative lies in the hands of the Senate. What’s your plan to seal the deal?", "likelihood": 1 } } } if (ans == 4524) { healthcare = 2; campaignTrail_temp.questions_json[8] = { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15844, "fields": { "priority": 1, "description": "As the battle for healthcare comes to pass, you realize you may have bit off more than you can chew. Your adherence to the Massachusetts plan has invited conservatives and the health insurance industry to put the system itself under a microscope, elevating spiraling healthcare costs in Massachusetts to an issue of national concern. As Dole and even conservative Democrats start to back away over concerns of costs and government overreach, what’s your plan to right the ship and save healthcare reform?", "likelihood": 1 } } } if (ans == 4525) { healthcare = 3; campaignTrail_temp.questions_json[8] = { "model": "campaign_trail.question", "pk": 15853, "fields": { "priority": 1, "description": "The tsunami triggered by your increasingly bloated, bureaucratic, over-engineered healthcare proposal threatens to consume your entire presidency. The health insurance industry has marshalled the troops in opposition to your agenda, working in tandem with congressional conservatives to stall your agenda on Capitol Hill while funding a nationwide ad campaign to rally the public against healthcare reform. Even Big Pharma is starting to back off, spooked by talk of price controls and regional alliances.
